programme.title: Wildest Islands: Falkland Islands: Penguin Paradise
programme.desc: The Falkland's bleak, battered, and windswept archipelago lies 460 km from the South American mainland. Rugged mountains and expansive pristine coastline are a unique home for wildlife  especially for one of the world's most iconic flightless birds.

programme.title: Australia's Wild Places: The Island of the Devil
programme.desc: Just 460,000 people live in Tasmania, an island off the southeast coast of mainland Australia. While its human population is small, the majestic state is rich in natural wonders and is known for its large tracts of pristine wilderness.

programme.title: Wildest Africa: Nile: An African Odyssey
programme.desc: The Nile is the longest river in the world. Ancient Egypt, one of the history's most powerful civilizations, depended hugely upon it. Each year, it flooded, replenishing the fields with fertile silt. This automatic irrigation fueled Egypt's economy.

programme.title: Australia's Wild Places: The Coast of the Whalesharks
programme.desc: On the remote coastline of Australia’s northwest, there are great natural wonders to be found. Above the sea on this sunbaked coast, there is very little; only a sparse landscape shaped by the arid conditions. But here, the miracle reveals itself…

programme.title: Wildest Africa: Namibia: Sands of Time
programme.desc: Namibia is an arid, rough land. However, it has been inhabited for thousands of years, by people as well as animals, and shows no sign of losing popularity with either.

programme.title: Wildest Africa: Virungas: Land of Ice and Fire
programme.desc: It's home to some of the world's most iconic animals  including the African great apes.
programme.sub-title: Virungas: Land of Ice and Fire

programme.title: Wildest Africa: Lake Turkana: Land of the Crocodile
programme.desc: The largest of Africa's Rift valley lakes, Lake Turkana is at the heart of one of the harshest landscapes on the planet. Despite its hostile environment, man and beast have both worked out a way to survive.
programme.sub-title: Lake Turkana: Land of the Crocodile

programme.title: Wildest Indochina: Southwest China: China's Secret Garden
programme.desc: Tucked away in China's southwest corner is a hidden oasis unlike any other. Snow-capped mountains, temperate valleys and tropical jungles are home to a spectacular array of wildlife.

programme.title: Wildest Islands: Galapagos: Darwin's Eden
programme.desc: The Galapagos archipelago contains 13 main islands and a hundred or so smaller rocks and reefs. The region's wilderness is so pristine and rich in life. / Lying like a teardrop in the Indian Ocean, Sri Lanka is an island that defies convention.

programme.title: Wildest India: Western Ghats: Monsoon Mountains
programme.desc: See how the Himalayas run across 2,500km of north India.The Himalayas' highlands offer little direct refuge to humans. /Then, learn about the western Ghats. A spine of mountains that lay claim to being one of the most bio-diverse places in the world.

programme.title: Wildest India: Thar Desert: Sacred Sands
programme.desc: Covering 200,000 km sq, India's Thar desert is one of the harshest places on Earth. Desiccating winds and near permanent drought has earned this unforgiving land another name: the region of death.

programme.title: Wildest Islands: Zanzibar: Land of Giants
programme.desc: The Zanzibar Archipelago has been labeled the most exotic island chain in the world. Pristine sands and azure blue sea make it a much loved holiday destination. /The Caribbean is a tropical paradise. Each of the 7,000 islands are unique.

programme.title: Australia's Wild Places: The Australian Alps
programme.desc: It may come as a surprise to non-Australians that the island continent renowned for its flat plains and searing deserts has snow-capped mountains at all.

programme.title: Wildest Middle East: Turkey: Life on the Edge
programme.desc: Turkey is a land of contrasts, where the wildlife faces extreme challenges. Mountains, altitude, climatic changes, hostile coastlines, and earthquakes all play their part but, somehow, the wildlife battles the odds and survives.

programme.title: Wildest Africa: Mount Kenya: African Heart of Ice
programme.desc: Mount Kenya soars over 5km into the air. It is the second highest mountain in Africa. At its summit, temperatures fluctuate from over 30 degrees celsius to below freezing.
programme.sub-title: Mount Kenya: African Heart of Ice

programme.title: Wildest Latin America: The Andes: World in the Clouds
programme.desc: The longest chain of mountains in the world, the Andes run the length of western South America and dictate the climate for the whole continent. They include some of the highest peaks outside the Himalayas.
programme.sub-title: The Andes: World in the Clouds

programme.title: Wildest Arctic: Taiga: The Frozen Forest
programme.desc: Containing one third of the world's trees, the frozen forests of the Taiga are the lungs of the planet. This great forest spans across shores of the Pacific, sheltering some of the Earth's most majestic animals, and some of its most secretive.

programme.title: Memoirs Of An Invisible Man
programme.desc: When a government experiment goes awry it renders an innocent bystander invisible.

programme.title: Cold Souls: Cold Souls
programme.desc: Paul is an actor who feels bogged down by his participation in a production of Chekov's play, Vanya.

programme.title: The Sentinel
programme.desc: Compromised by his affair with the first lady, a veteran secret service agent is framed as a potential presidential assassin and must rely on a hostile former prot‚g‚ to flush out the real culprit.

programme.title: Spy Next Door
programme.desc: A former CIA agent hoping to settle down with his girlfriend uses an opportunity to babysit her children as a chance to win them over. However, things take a dramatic turn when an old enemy shows up, forcing him back into his old lifestyle.

programme.title: In Time
programme.desc: Justin Timberlake and Amanda Seyfried co-star in this sci-fi thriller set in a future world where aging stops at 25, and the only way for one man to stay alive is to steal more time.

programme.title: The Last Witch Hunter
programme.desc: The last witch hunter (Vin Diesel) is all that stands between humanity and the combined forces of the most horrifying witches in history.

programme.title: The Big Short
programme.desc: Based on the true story of four outsiders who saw what the big banks, media, and government refused to: the global collapse of the economy. A bold investment leads them into the dark underbelly of banking, where everyone and everything is in question.

programme.title: Snitch
programme.desc: A father (Dwayne Johnson) goes undercover for the DEA in order to free his son who was imprisoned after being set up in a drug deal.

programme.title: Benji
programme.desc: Two school kids strike up a friendship with an orphaned puppy named Benji. When danger befalls them and they end up kidnapped by robbers who are in over their heads, Benji and his scruffy sidekick come to the rescue.

programme.title: Hercules And The Captive Women
programme.desc: Hercules and the Captive Women: Hercules tries to save his son from an evil queen.

programme.title: Last Of The Wild Horses
programme.desc: A cowboy winds up in the middle of a turf war between a rancher and his neighbors in Last of the Wild Horses.

programme.title: The Phantom Planet
programme.desc: When US astronaut Captain Frank Chapman’s military spaceship is damaged by asteroids, he lands safely on the giant asteroid Rheton.

programme.title: Hamlet
programme.desc: A dour production of Shakespeare’s play produced for German TV. A prince returns home for his father’s funeral and doesn’t like what he finds.

programme.title: Ring of Terror
programme.desc: A medical student (played by George B. Mather) is tormented by a frat-house hazing after being asked to remove a ring from the finger of a corpse.

programme.title: The Sword And The Dragon
programme.desc: A Russian hero plots an invasion against the Mongols in The Sword and the Dragon. Mike and the Bots create their own topical comedy show, reenact the table cloth scene from the movie, and try their hand at playing Dungeons and Dragons.

programme.title: The Black Scorpion
programme.desc: Giant scorpions rise out of a volcano and attack Mexico in The Black Scorpion.

programme.title: The Human Duplicators
programme.desc: An alien takes over a professor's mansion-basement laboratory, and makes android clones to infiltrate the government.

programme.title: Red Zone Cuba
programme.desc: An escaped convict and his two pals take part in the Bay of Pigs invasion, then return home with a plan to get rich.

programme.title: Cave Dwellers
programme.desc: Newly-married wife Jenni can't tell if the skull she sees in her new home is a ghostly apparition of her husband's dead first wife, hallucinations from her own nervous condition... or something else.

programme.title: Camp Lakebottom: Slugfight / Izomborg
programme.desc: The campers must travel back in time to prevent Grandpappy Black Butt from stealing the deed.

programme.title: Camp Lakebottom: Head Two Head / The Last of the Wild
programme.desc: Suzi and Gretchen get fused into a two-headed Gruzi!
programme.sub-title: Head Two Head / The Last of the Wild

programme.title: Camp Lakebottom: Sweat, Hot Lakebottom Summer / The S'Mogre
programme.desc: A heat wave forces McGee to sneak a dab of Sawyer's anti-sweat cream. After trying to cheer up Armand, McGee creates a s'more so big, it topples and coats him in delicious goo.
programme.sub-title: Sweat, Hot Lakebottom Summer / The S'Mogre

programme.title: Camp Lakebottom: Seven Foot itch / Being McGee
programme.desc: Squirt ressurects his pet lousy from the grave.

programme.title: Camp Lakebottom: Camp Plantbottom / Game Over
programme.desc: It's Suzi's birthday and McGee ate her gift!

programme.title: Camp Lakebottom: McGee's First Flush / House of Ear Wax
programme.desc: Two of McGee and Suzi's former pets "Ocean Apes" return for vengeance and are mutated in all kinds of horrible ways! Squirt's chronic ear wax issues escalate as his giant ball of wax seems to take on a mind of its own.
programme.sub-title: McGee's First Flush / House of Ear Wax

programme.title: Camp Lakebottom: Ice Queen / Live and Let Squatch
programme.desc: When the campers try to reclaim their stolen Lakebottom items.
programme.sub-title: Ice Queen / Live and Let Squatch

programme.title: Camp Lakebottom: Marshmallow Madness / 28 Suzi's Later
programme.desc: Armand goes into a feeding frenzy when wild marshmallows give the campers a rash.

programme.title: Camp Lakebottom: Ring Around The Gretchen / Chilli Con Carnage
programme.desc: Beauty pageant Gretchen looks to connect with her girly side.
programme.sub-title: Ring Around The Gretchen / Chilli Con Carnage

programme.title: Camp Lakebottom: The Day Squirt Stood Still / Anti Gravity
programme.desc: The campers track down a UFO inhabited by an alien from the planet Squipiter.
programme.sub-title: The Day Squirt Stood Still / Anti Gravity

programme.title: Camp Lakebottom: Fright Club / Bottomdome
programme.desc: The campers discover an enchanted flashlight.

programme.title: Camp Lakebottom: Golfadoom / There is Something About Mamba
programme.desc: McGee, Gretchen and Buttsquat must beat Haggis MacJockstrap in golf!
programme.sub-title: Golfadoom / There is Something About Mamba

programme.title: Camp Lakebottom: Camp Lockdownbottom / All You Need is Larva
programme.desc: Rosebud is sick of everyone borrowing her stuff, and draws the line when her precious golden ladle goes missing. After eating raw cookie dough, Squirt develops a case of worms!
programme.sub-title: Camp Lockdownbottom / All You Need is Larva

programme.title: Camp Lakebottom: Meet The Gretch's Parents / Now with 100% More Portal
programme.desc: McGee and Squirt are flabbergasted when Gretchen's parents turn out to be the famous action-star couple, the Gritchersons! The kids discover an interdimensional portal to travel back and forth between parallel Lakebottom dimensions.
programme.sub-title: Meet The Gretch's Parents / Now with 100% More Portal

programme.title: Camp Lakebottom: Cheeks of Dread / Doo Doo Doomsday
programme.desc: Gretchen must confront her greatest and cutest fear.
programme.sub-title: Cheeks of Dread / Doo Doo Doomsday

programme.title: Camp Lakebottom: Are You My Mommy? / Slimey Come Home
programme.desc: The Campers and Buttsquat come across an ancient pyramid inhabited by mummy's.
programme.sub-title: Are You My Mommy? / Slimey Come Home

programme.title: Camp Lakebottom: Tooth Troll / Hive and Seek
programme.desc: Squirt is captured by a Tooth Troll!

programme.title: Camp Lakebottom: Cabin Fever / It's a Horrible Life
programme.desc: While lost in the woods, McGee, Gretchen, Squirt and Sawyer discover a creepy cabin.
programme.sub-title: Cabin Fever / It's a Horrible Life

programme.title: Camp Lakebottom: Big Top Terror / Slimal Fear
programme.desc: The campers summon Armand's old rival, Doofus the clown who vows to destroy the campers.

programme.title: Camp Lakebottom: Pod Parents / Zombie Scouts
programme.desc: Suzi, desperate to get McGee sent home, invites her parents for a visit to Camp Lakebottom.

programme.title: Camp Lakebottom: High Plains Garbage Eater / Dream a Little Scream
programme.desc: The gang finds themselves in the middle of a good ol' fashioned show down!
programme.sub-title: High Plains Garbage Eater / Dream a Little Scream

programme.title: Camp Lakebottom: Jaws of Old Toothy / Arachnattack
programme.desc: When Sawyer closes the beach because of a giant fish, McGee vows to rid the lake of "Old Toothy".

programme.title: Camp Lakebottom: Trouble In Spit Creek / The Spy Who Squatched Me
programme.desc: During a game of Stink Toss, McGee and Buttsquat get lost in the forest for what feels like days!
programme.sub-title: Trouble In Spit Creek / The Spy Who Squatched Me

programme.title: Camp Lakebottom: Scare-A-Normal-Activity / Beast Feast
programme.desc: McGee vows to protect Lakebottom's secrets from the prying host of 'Paranormal Confronter.'

programme.title: Camp Lakebottom: Pirate of Ickygloomy / Attack of the 50-Foot Squirt
programme.desc: While playing Pirates on a shipwreck the Bottom Dwellers must battle the ghost Spitbeard the Pirate.
programme.sub-title: Pirate of Ickygloomy / Attack of the 50-Foot Squirt

programme.title: Camp Lakebottom: Duh'Rehka / Chore Leave
programme.desc: While chasing his gopher-nemesis, Sawyer gets zapped into his brain bin and emerges super smart!

programme.title: Camp Lakebottom: Terror form the Toybox / Slimeball Run
programme.desc: Buttsquat's Teddy Bear washes up at Camp Lakebottom and hypnotizes the Bottom Dwellers.
programme.sub-title: Terror form the Toybox / Slimeball Run

programme.title: Camp Lakebottom: Pranks for Nothing / Zombie Dearest
programme.desc: Eager to regain his lost prank mojo, McGee releases a mischievous pixy named Hanky Pranky.

programme.title: Camp Lakebottom: Mc Crossroads / Lucky Duck
programme.desc: McGee signs a contract with Sheba the bandshee.

programme.title: Camp Lakebottom: Who's Ghouling Who? / The Adominable Dr. Squatch
programme.desc: McGee accidentally summons Percy, a ghost with a very 'fiery' personality.
programme.sub-title: Who's Ghouling Who? / The Adominable Dr. Squatch

programme.title: Camp Lakebottom: Ants In Our Camp / Fanboy Freakout
programme.desc: McGee mistakenly concocts a spray that shrinks the campers to a microscopic size.
programme.sub-title: Ants In Our Camp / Fanboy Freakout

programme.title: Camp Lakebottom: Stage Fright / Frankenfixer
programme.desc: The Bottom Dwellers stage Armand's awful play but a jealous Suzi unleashes a curse on the production.

programme.title: Camp Lakebottom: Bird Brains / The Real Vincent
programme.desc: Squirt is eager to assist his idol, Dr. Sapstein on his latest quest for the rare mocker bird.

programme.title: Camp Lakebottom: Hiccups / Boogeyman Fever
programme.desc: Things start disappearing around Lakebottom!

programme.title: Camp Lakebottom: Clockwork Slime / Ride the Haunted Howler
programme.desc: The Campers find a time travelling clock on the beach!
programme.sub-title: Clockwork Slime / Ride the Haunted Howler

programme.title: Camp Lakebottom: Valley of the Iguanasquat / Legend of Wiggly's Gold
programme.desc: When the campers discover a prehistoric valley near Stinky Pits they also find a cursed gem.
programme.sub-title: Valley of the Iguanasquat / Legend of Wiggly's Gold

programme.title: Camp Lakebottom: Adventures in Bottomland / Golem My Way?
programme.desc: During an unfair polo match, McGee falls down an armadillo and wakes up in 'Bottomland.'
programme.sub-title: Adventures in Bottomland / Golem My Way?

programme.title: Camp Lakebottom: Bloody Marty / Ghost In The Mower
programme.desc: The kids ignore Rosebuds warning and summon Bloody Marty from the ominous mirror world.
programme.sub-title: Bloody Marty / Ghost In The Mower

programme.title: The Simple Life
programme.desc: Paris and Nicole are paired with a hard-to-please husband.

programme.title: The Simple Life
programme.desc: Paris and Nicole visit an artistic family with varied talents.

programme.title: The Simple Life
programme.desc: Paris can’t stand to spend another day as a housewife and hires an impersonator to take her place, giving Nicole an opportunity to exact her revenge.

programme.title: The Simple Life
programme.desc: Paris Hilton and Nicole Richie say goodbye to their jet-setting lifestyle in Los Angeles, and move in with the Ledings, a family in Altus, Arkansas.

programme.title: The Simple Life
programme.desc: Paris and Nicole are hired to work at a dairy farm operated by Albert’s no-nonsense friend, but after tiring of the hard work and smell, they head for a nearby hot tub… and end up getting fired.

programme.title: The Simple Life
programme.desc: Paris and Nicole are hired to work at a fast-food restaurant, but they’re bored and frustrated by the demands of the job, and soon rebel by pulling pranks during each of their assignments; when the Ledings reprimand them, the girls just go out dancing.

programme.title: The Simple Life
programme.desc: Paris and Nicole agree to be honorary co-chairs of the Springtime Gala, but instead of working at the gala, they engage in their usual shocking behavior; the Ledings are embarrassed, and everyone in town is gossiping about Paris and Nicole.

programme.title: The Simple Life
programme.desc: The girls go to work for a cattle auctioneer, but when they’re sent to buy cattle feed, they charge several other items without permission; the auctioneer comes after them for the money, and the girl try to lie their way out of the debt.

programme.title: The Simple Life
programme.desc: Paris and Nicole finally get a job they enjoy: working at a gas station, where there are plenty of guys to flirt with; but a confrontation erupts when Albert and Janet tell the girls to stay away from the small town boys, and the girls disobey them.

programme.title: The Simple Life
programme.desc: Paris and Nicole reminisce about their month in Altus, make amends, and say goodbye to the people they’ve come to know before returning to Los Angeles.

programme.title: The Simple Life
programme.desc: Leeza Gibbons hosts a look back at Paris’ and Nicole’s stay in Altus, Arkansas, as the girls reunite with the Leding family in front of a studio audience.

programme.title: The Simple Life
programme.desc: Paris and Nicole depart from Miami Beach toward their first destination, the Batten Family Ranch.

programme.title: The Simple Life
programme.desc: Paris and Nicole get jobs at Weeki Wachee Springs in Florida and play a surprising game of hide-and-seek with their boss's daughter.

programme.title: The Simple Life
programme.desc: Paris and Nicole work as maids at the Caliente Nudist Colony and learn the meaning of the word "termination."

programme.title: The Simple Life
programme.desc: While staying with a host family, Nicole butts heads with their oldest son and decides to give him a taste of his own medicine.

programme.title: The Simple Life
programme.desc: In the swamplands of Louisiana, Paris and Nicole meet their next host family and make a big impression after taking their daughter on a shopping spree; the women get jobs trapping crawfish.

programme.title: The Simple Life
programme.desc: A stop in Texas brings Paris and Nicole to their new host family; the women convince the Lutz-Carillos boys to sneak out with them to go tanning.

programme.title: The Simple Life
programme.desc: Paris and Nicole adjust to a rule against profanity in the household of their new hosts, devout members of a gospel choir; the pair get jobs with a minor league baseball team.

programme.title: Mob Wives: Episode 5
programme.desc: The ladies enjoy a wild VIP party, Renee undergoes a painful beauty treatment, and Karen and Drita's long-simmering conflict over Lee finally erupts into a heated argument.

programme.title: Mob Wives: Episode 6
programme.desc: Drita does a glamorous photoshoot for Lee, Renee has a new business idea, and when the four women gather for a dinner party, their attempts at conflict resolution go disastrously awry.

programme.title: Mob Wives: Episode 7
programme.desc: The ladies make peace in the wake of the tumultuous dinner, Karen reunites with her daughter Karina, Carla prepares for Joe's release from prison, and Drita urges Renee to try online dating.

programme.title: Mob Wives: Episode 8
programme.desc: Karen is stung by harsh public reactions to her book project, Drita deals with some unwanted guests, and Renee's friends are frustrated by her lingering relationship with her unreliable ex

programme.title: Cheaters: Episode 44
programme.desc: Ronnie Warren fights his fiance's young lover. Follow up with Dana Christopher.

programme.title: Cheaters: Episode 1
programme.desc: Michael is concerned his wife is having an internet affair. Follow up with Charles Hughes.

programme.title: Cheaters: Episode 2
programme.desc: Jeff is deceived by his girlfriend. Follow up with Chadelle Gomberg.

programme.title: Cheaters: Episode 3
programme.desc: April learns her lover is unfaithful. Follow up with Steve Hauser.

programme.title: Cheaters: Episode 4
programme.desc: Amy learns the truth about her boyfriend. Follow up with Deedee Sampson.

programme.title: Cheaters: Episode 5
programme.desc: Marvin realizes the truth about his girlfriend. Follow up with Johnathan Shaw.

programme.title: Cheaters: Episode 6
programme.desc: A married man deceives Crystol. Follow up with Curtis Jackson.

programme.title: Cheaters: Episode 7
programme.desc: Brian is suspicious of his girlfriend's behavior. Follow up with Mari Gomez.

programme.title: Cheaters: Episode 8
programme.desc: Marty surprises Jennifer with two secrets. Follow up with Marie Mendez.

programme.title: Cheaters: Episode 9
programme.desc: Brittany is betrayed by her girlfriend. Follow up with Judith Hughes.

programme.title: Cheaters: Episode 10
programme.desc: Two loved ones deceive Sandra Sanchez. Follow up with Kacey Becker.

programme.title: Cheaters: Episode 11

programme.title: Undercover Boss Canada
programme.desc: Kristen Flynn, the boss of Premiere Van Lines. She’s a woman with a formidable reputation. Her own staff call her “Frugal Flynn” because she watches the pennies to protect profit.

programme.title: MythBusters: Episode 49
programme.desc: Myths tested: Can a football fly farther if it is filled with helium? Is it possible for a human to catch a bullet in his teeth?

programme.title: MythBusters: Episode 52
programme.desc: Myths tested: Can a bullet fired straight up in the air kill someone on the way back down? If low-end vodka is charcoal-filtered, will it become a top-shelf vodka?

programme.title: MythBusters: Episode 164
programme.desc: Can honey bees lift a laptop? Can hitting a bug at high speeds on a motorcycle be fatal? Can hanging a bag of water repel flies?

programme.title: MythBusters: Episode 223
programme.desc: Do males prefer women with blonde hair? Do pheromone sprays work? Are women attracted to wealthy men? Do men give larger tips to women with larger breasts?

programme.title: MythBusters: Episode 95
programme.desc: Myths tested: Can a person's eyes pop out while sneezing with eyes open? Can shifting to reverse stop a runaway car? Can three cigarette butts fired from a black-powder rifle kill a person?

programme.title: MythBusters: Episode 47
programme.desc: Myths tested: Can a deadly crossbow be made from materials available to prison inmates? Can vodka remove the smell of cigarettes from clothes or kill bees like an insecticide?

programme.title: MythBusters: Episode 25
programme.desc: Myths tested: Is it possible to make a catapult with a boom-lift cherry-picker? Does a person save more gas by driving with the AC on instead of having the windows down?

programme.title: MythBusters: Episode 44
programme.desc: Myths tested: Can two semi-trucks collide head-on and completely crush a compact car? Can vodka cure foot odor or bad breath?

programme.title: MythBusters: Episode 185
programme.desc: Is it possible to re-inflate a flat tire and re-seat it on its rim? How effective are various handgun firing stances?

programme.title: MythBusters: Episode 142
programme.desc: Can antacid tablets be used to blow a prison cell open? Can a person drive safely on a moonless night without using his headlights?

programme.title: MythBusters: Episode 187
programme.desc: If a pressure-triggered bomb is cooled with liquid nitrogen, can its detonation be delayed long enough for a person to seek protection?

programme.title: MythBusters: Episode 195
programme.desc: Can you survive a 35-foot fall while covered in bubble wrap? Can a rocket-powered ejector seat flip an upside-down car back onto its wheels, as seen in "Die Another Day"?

programme.title: MythBusters: Episode 189
programme.desc: Can a vehicle run as well on square wheels as it does on round ones? Can two cars stuck nose to nose really do the maneuvers shown in the film "Date Night?"

programme.title: MythBusters: Episode 38
programme.desc: Myths tested: Is it possible to slingshot a person over the border and onto a mattress?

programme.title: MythBusters: Episode 173
programme.desc: Did the Syrians design a water-skimming torpedo as early as the 13th century? If a truckload of wine catches fire, will the bottles blow their corks 100 feet and sound like a machine gun?

programme.title: MythBusters: Episode 100
programme.desc: Myths tested: Can a water heater explode and shoot through the roof of a house? If a person is being dragged by a horse, can the friction caused by the movement make the jeans catch on fire?

programme.title: MythBusters: Episode 90
programme.desc: Myths tested: Can someone's hat be shot off? Is it possible to break someone out of jail with horses? Can an airbag turn a lock-pick into a lethal spear?

programme.title: 12 In A Box
programme.desc: 12 In A Box is the laugh-out-loud story of 12 former school friends who get together for a reunion they will never forget. Staring Miranda Hart.

programme.title: Memoirs Of An Invisible Man
programme.desc: When a government experiment goes awry it renders an innocent bystander invisible.

programme.title: Shortcut to Happiness
programme.desc: A down-and-out writer sells his soul to the devil in exchange for fame and fortune. Starring: Alec Baldwin, Anthony Hopkins, Kim Cattrall, Dan Aykroyd, Jennifer Love Hewitt

programme.title: Mortdecai
programme.desc: Depp, Paltrow, and McGregor star in this globe-trotting comedy about an unlikely team recruited to find a stolen, priceless painting.

programme.title: Simply Irresistible
programme.desc: Magic and laughter are the main ingredients in this SIMPLY IRRESISTIBLE love story of a restaurant owner/chef and a department store executive who seem to have nothing in common.

programme.title: King Of California
programme.desc: Commercial airline pilot Whip Whitaker (Denzel Washington) has a problem with drugs and alcohol, though so far he's managed to complete his flights safely.

programme.title: The Big Wedding
programme.desc: A modern family tries to survive a weekend wedding celebration that has the potential to become a full-blown fiasco in this uproarious romantic comedy with an all-star cast.

programme.title: Aloha
programme.desc: A celebrated military contractor returns to the site of his greatest career triumphs and re-connects with a long-ago love while unexpectedly falling for the hard-charging Air Force watchdog assigned to him.

programme.title: School for Scoundrels
programme.desc: A young guy short on luck enrolls in a class to build confidence to help win over the girl of his dreams, which becomes complicated when his teacher has the same agenda.

programme.title: Cold Souls: Cold Souls
programme.desc: Paul is an actor who feels bogged down by his participation in a production of Chekov's play, Vanya.

programme.title: Count Duckula: Around the World in a Total Daze

programme.title: Danger Mouse

programme.title: Count Duckula: The Count and the Pauper

programme.title: Danger Mouse

programme.sub-title: The Good the Bad and the Motionless

programme.title: Danger Mouse
programme.desc: Penfold and Danger Mouse journey to the center of the Earth to stop a loud noise that originates there.

programme.title: Count Duckula: Autoduck

programme.title: Danger Mouse
programme.desc: Professor Squakencluck shows off his time machine. However, when Penfold gets too close to the machine's targeted area, he is sucked into the past, 150 million years ago, in exchange for a dinosaur egg. Danger Mouse heads back to look for him.

programme.title: Danger Mouse
programme.desc: Danger Mouse and Penfold go after a mad composer who has destroyed all the world's music.

programme.title: Danger Mouse
programme.desc: Danger Mouse and Penfold go to the Amazon jungle where a tribe have been sacrificing people to The Great Ant God from Ataxia. Penfold is captured by the tribe and his "magic caterpillars" (eyebrows) are to be sacrificed.

programme.title: Count Duckula: Mutinous Penguins
programme.desc: Lost in the Arctic seas, Duckula, Igor and Nanny must survive in order to find their home.

programme.title: Danger Mouse
programme.desc: Colonel K thinks Danger Mouse has camouflaged himself and starts rambling. After Danger Mouse arrives to straighten up things, Danger Mouse and Penfold head to Canada to track down a Bigfoot that has been crushing the local villages and landscape.

programme.title: Danger Mouse
programme.desc: A giant spaceship is spotted heading for Earth. It's part of the "Intergalactic 147" snooker game and it wants to pot the Earth into a black hole. This is the series finale.

programme.title: Danger Mouse
programme.desc: Penfold takes a long time to have his passport photos taken at a booth, but he gets a sheet that says "You have broken the lens.

programme.title: Danger Mouse
programme.desc: Greenback attempts to take over England with his latest invention...an army of remote control, allergy causing pillows.

programme.title: Danger Mouse
programme.desc: Baron Greenback uses a shrink ray to enter Colonel K's body to learn all of the Colonel's secrets. Danger Mouse and Penfold follow, but they get diverted because Greenback changed some of the signs. They also have to deal with the body guarding cells.

programme.title: Count Duckula: The Great Ducktective
programme.desc: Goosewing tries to frame Duckula for a relative's murder.

programme.title: Danger Mouse
programme.desc: Danger Mouse and Penfold find Baron Greenback's hideout, but the Baron has escaped, and Stiletto is left behind in disguise.

programme.title: Count Duckula: Bombay Duck

programme.title: Count Duckula: Amnesiac Duck

programme.desc: Count Duckula and his servants travel onto a steamboat in the River Mississippi.

programme.desc: Gaston and Pierre and Von Goosewing break into Castle Duckula to steal two vases.

programme.title: Danger Mouse
programme.desc: Greenback has placed a large number of sand pyramids all over England, threatening to sink the island Danger Mouse travels to the Sahara Desert to stop him.

programme.title: Count Duckula: The Show Must Go On

programme.title: Danger Mouse
programme.desc: Washing machines from all over London have busted out of their residences and are now under the control of Baron Greenback, who orders them to assemble and to attack Danger Mouse.

programme.title: Danger Mouse
programme.desc: Greenback has London at a standstill after he steals all of the world's tea.

programme.title: Danger Mouse

programme.title: Count Duckula: A Family Reunion
programme.desc: Duckula hosts the family reunion but he does not like it one bit as he knows that his relatives will kill him if they discover that he's not a real vampire.

programme.title: Count Duckula: Arctic Circles
programme.desc: Duckula travels to the North Pole.

programme.title: Danger Mouse
programme.desc: Danger Mouse and Penfold are in Hong Kong where they are trying to arrest Baron Greenback.

programme.title: Danger Mouse
programme.desc: Danger Mouse and Penfold are sent to find Merlin's magic inkwell which will grant the finder a wish. They go on a treasure hunt by following a series of clues.
programme.sub-title: Where There's a Well There's a Way

programme.title: Danger Mouse
programme.desc: Danger Mouse and Penfold track Greenback down to a New York City skyscraper run by International Oddbods and a guy who wears a bag over his head. Inside, there are all sorts of bizarre things going on.

programme.title: Count Duckula: Hi-Duck
programme.desc: Duckula, Igor and Nanny take an airplane to Nice.

programme.title: Count Duckula

programme.desc: A letter arrives at Castle Duckula sent by lawyers in London.

programme.title: Danger Mouse
programme.desc: Due to a fluke in the narrator's microphone, Danger Mouse and Penfold are transported to the year 1215, where they have to act out characters from Robin Hood.

programme.title: Laurel & Hardy: Block Heads
programme.desc: Unaware that the Great War has ended, Stan remains faithfully at his post for twenty years

programme.title: Laurel & Hardy: Busy Bodies

programme.title: Elvira's Haunted Hills

programme.title: The Shape of Things to Come

programme.desc: The criminal exploits of Public Enemy number 1, George 'Machine-Gun' Kelly, during the 1930s.

programme.title: Candy Stripe Nurses
programme.desc: Marisa, Dianne and Sandy are three sexy teenagers who work as candy stripers at a local hospital.

programme.title: Wonderwall
programme.desc: Oscar Collins, an eccentric, absent-minded professor, becomes obsessed with his new neighbors, Penny Lane and her boyfriend. He drills holes in the walls and ceiling to spy on her day and night, lost in fantasies and daydreams about the woman next door.

programme.title: Summer of Fear

A teenage girl's(Linda Blair) life is turned upside down after her cousin moves into her house, and as time goes by she begins to suspect that she may be a practitioner of witchcraft.

programme.title: Deadly Women
programme.desc: Blood ties are strong ties, sometimes strong enough to pull a person into a murderous conspiracy. When a family member wields his or her power, the result can be death.

programme.title: Deadly Women
programme.desc: The heaviest judgments of convicted killers are reserved for those who committed premeditated murder. These women plot and plan. They know the consequences of their actions, but still they go forward to kill.

programme.title: Deadly Women
programme.desc: These women never get their hands dirty: no blood touched them because they manipulate others to kill for them...but they didn't get away.

programme.title: Deadly Women
programme.desc: Behind lace-curtained windows and beneath sensible haircuts lurked evil. Appearances were deceptive. We did not notice these female killers walking by. These women wore a mask of sanity.

programme.title: Deadly Women
programme.desc: We want to believe that all women would give their lives to protect their children. But there are horrifying cases when a mother will turn against her natural instinct and kill her own flesh and blood.

programme.title: Deadly Women
programme.desc: The path towards revenge can sometimes lead to murder. It can be quick, fiery and violent. It can also be a dish served cold, the product of a calm, calculated plot.

programme.title: Deadly Women
programme.desc: A lovely lady with an innocent smile, kind eyes, a charming personality? No one would suspect a woman like that would murder. These women don’t look or behave like killers.

programme.title: Deadly Women
programme.desc: No one doubts a woman’s capacity to love, but sometimes lust can turn lethal. In the ultimate fatal attraction, "Deadly Women" looks at cases of murder driven by obsessive love.

programme.title: Deadly Women
programme.desc: Nurse Beverley Allitt overdosed multiple infants, Stella Nickell poisoned medicine capsules in stores and Kathleen Follbigg killed four of her own children.

programme.title: Deadly Women: Kill For Cash
programme.desc: Money is a strong motivator. It can be used for good. It can be used for evil. It can be the driving force behind murder. These women would stop at nothing to get their hands on the cash they needed.

programme.title: Deadly Women: Lover's Revenge
programme.desc: These women burn madly with anger so deep they'll kill to satisfy it. What makes them turn on the ones they love? Find out as we explore the reasons for a lover's revenge.

programme.title: Deadly Women: Lethal Love
programme.desc: Love is a powerful emotion, one that sits in the core of our soul and fills our lives with hope. But when love turns sour, the results can be deadly. In an act of revenge, these deadly women unite with one common goal, to kill.

programme.title: Deadly Women: Senseless Slayings
programme.desc: When a person places no value on human life, they can become unstoppable in their twisted missions to destroy the lives around them. These women all pushed boundaries, and shattered lives with their actions.

programme.title: Deadly Women: Greed
programme.desc: Known as “black widows,” these women kill their spouses, lovers and children for profit. Murder for them becomes a business. Their trade is murder. Their profit? Blood money.

programme.title: Deadly Women: To Love and to Murder
programme.desc: Marriage is a lifelong commitment of love, and wedding vows. 'To have and to hold; for better, for worse; until death do us part'. But for these monstrous wives, it was only the vow of 'death do us part' they upheld.

programme.title: Deadly Women: Sins of the Sister
programme.desc: A sisterly bond can mean a life full of friendship and sibling love. But for some women, having a sister means nothing more then having their own personal meal ticket, scapegoat and combatant. These types of relationships can lead to deadly outcomes.

programme.title: Deadly Women
programme.desc: Love and desire are powerful currents that surge through human affairs. When they run off course, the results can be deadly.

programme.title: Deadly Women: Breaking Point
programme.desc: Anger, fear and despair are basic human emotions. But when these emotions are coupled with a broken mind, a perfect storm is created. Teetering on the edge of unbalance, these women resort to murder when pushed to the breaking point.

programme.title: Deadly Women: Under His Control
programme.desc: These are love stories with bleak, bloody endings. Blinded by desire, obsessed and malleable, some women fall into lethal relationships with dangerous, controlling men.

programme.title: The FBI Files: Dixie Mafia
programme.desc: On Mississippi's Gulf Coast, a Judge and his politician wife are murdered in their home. The killer left few clues. It looked like a professional hit, and the investigation led nowhere. But the FBI refused to give up.

programme.title: The FBI Files: Deadly Threat
programme.desc: A suspected drug dealer would stop at nothing to keep local and federal authorities from building a case against him. When his organization started targeting the government itself, federal agencies went on high alert.

programme.title: The FBI Files: Without Remorse
programme.desc: A small town without any crime was turned inside out by the arrival of one dangerous stranger. Some killers are cold and calculating; others are impulsive, unpredictable, and hard to pin down.

programme.title: The FBI Files: Dishonored
programme.desc: Shirley Russell was determined enough to reach the rank of Captain in the United States Marine Corps. When this officer turned up missing, her colleagues refused to believe she had gone AWOL. It was unclear whether Captain Shirley Russell was alive.

programme.title: The FBI Files: The Killing Zone
programme.desc: In the 1990's the war on drugs was in full swing. The front lines were often America's inner cities where gangs use violence and murder to rule their empires.

programme.title: The FBI Files: Crime Spree
programme.desc: They swept through six states like a plague, thrill killers acting without rhyme or reason. They targeted adults and young girls, kidnapping, and murdering. And in their rampage, they left plenty of clues, but moving targets are the hardest to hit.

programme.title: The FBI Files: The World Trade Center Bombing
programme.desc: Terrorist bombings are what happen in other countries. Not here in the United States. And yet, in 1993, it happened in New York City.

programme.title: The FBI Files: Human Prey
programme.desc: Thomas Dillon killed five outdoorsmen at random over a period of three years in rural southern Ohio. After a tip led the FBI to the murder weapon, ballistic testing made a match.

programme.title: The FBI Files: The C-11 Squad
programme.desc: New York City is not stranger to gang violence. But when a 12-year-old child was kidnapped, the gangs had crossed a line. Law enforcement raced to find the missing boy before it was too late.

programme.title: The FBI Files
programme.desc: A daring kidnapping and a bizarre ransom note baffled Mississippi authorities. The victim was the wife of a wealthy businessman. A list of 12 names and a few drops of blood were the only clues to her fate.

programme.title: The FBI Files: Deadly Business
programme.desc: Many escape the poverty of India to start new lives in the United States. One turned the American dream into a nightmare. People started turning up dead in New York's Indian community-authorities struggled to link fraud and murder to a powerful man.

programme.title: The FBI Files: Blood Brothers
programme.desc: When the small community of Noel, Missouri discovered their bank was robbed, they're shocked. When the bank president turned up missing, they're outraged. And when clues began to surface the facts were more terrible than anyone could have guessed.

programme.title: The FBI Files: The Murdering Cowboy
programme.desc: The sun set on the Wild West long ago, but some people refused to notice. Claude Dallas was one of them. A wily trapper, Dallas typified the rugged individualism that forged this country. He went too far when he killed two game wardens in 1981.

programme.title: The FBI Files: The Predator
programme.desc: In the early 1990's, Washington, D.C., became the murder capital of the nation. Few were left untouched by gang violence, which was ignited by the drug trade. By 1994, that violence would strike at the very heart of law enforcement.

programme.title: The FBI Files: Dead Run
programme.desc: In Pennsylvania, an inmate escaped with the help of his female accomplice. Following a trail of fraudulent credit card receipts and deaths, authorities track the pair, but always find themselves one step behind.

programme.title: The FBI Files: Deadly Stranger
programme.desc: The body of a young woman found burned in a truck launched a manhunt to find her killer. When more bodies began to turn up across the country, the FBI believed the cases were linked. Investigators had a suspect. They just couldn't find him.

programme.title: Worzel Gummidge Down Under
programme.desc: Worzel's heads are finally sent on from England. Unfortunately, when the trunk label is eaten by a goat, they end up in the local folk museum - alongside Aunt Sally and a vintage fairground organ.
programme.sub-title: Ten Heads Are Better Than One

programme.title: Worzel Gummidge Down Under
programme.desc: Worzel is hot on the heels of Travelling Scarecrowmaker but struggling to contend with his dark magic. Their encounter begins when Worzel is distracted by a funfair: can he reach Aunt Sally in time, and rescue her without his brave head?

programme.title: Worzel Gummidge Down Under
programme.desc: The Travelling Scarecrow Maker seeks his revenge by kidnapping Worzel and transforming him into a Slave Scarecrow. With Aunt Sally next in his sights, can his evil plan be thwarted?

programme.title: Worzel Gummidge Down Under
programme.desc: The Crowman recruits a reluctant Aunt Sally to rescue the enchanted Worzel. The task requires her to be a master of disguise to outwit the Travelling Scarecrow Maker - is she up to the challenge?

programme.title: Worzel Gummidge Down Under
programme.desc: Worzel makes preparations for his Birthday Tea Dance. However, he soon discovers the guest list may prove distinctly lacking in attendees. Can The Crowman, Mickey and Manu come to the rescue?

programme.title: Worzel Gummidge Down Under
programme.desc: Worzel's continuing efforts to win over Aunt Sally encounter a new obstacle when the Provincial Travelling Ballet troupe arrives in town.

programme.title: Worzel Gummidge Down Under
programme.desc: An impoverished Crowman is pursued by the bailiffs. Mickey and Manu decide to help but Worzel's efforts leave him stuck in an abandoned mine and, with the Crowman now retired, how will he escape?

programme.title: Worzel Gummidge Down Under
programme.desc: Mickey, Manu and The Crowman prepare a funeral for the missing Worzel. The unfortunate scarecrow is still in the clutches of the Prospector who is finding him to be a less than helpful assistant.

programme.title: Worzel Gummidge Down Under
programme.desc: When Aunt Sally sees a poster for a beauty contest, she goes shopping for a posh dress, a 'tiararara' and a makeover. Alas, the beauty contest isn't all that it seemed.

programme.title: Worzel Gummidge Down Under
programme.desc: The Crowman has a new scarecrow who he assigns to Worzel to show him the ropes. Unfortunately, the new recruit proves to be rather reluctant which only gets worse when the crop sprayers arrive.

programme.title: Worzel Gummidge Down Under
programme.desc: When one of The Crowman's previous creations returns to visit his maker, Worzel develops a nasty case of the green-eyed monster - which only gets worse when Weevily Swede meets his Aunt Sally.

programme.title: Worzel Gummidge Down Under
programme.desc: Aunt Sally's greedy dreams of wealth lead Worzel, Mickey and Manu into trouble once more when she helps a waxwork pirate escape from a funfair side-show, in search of his long-buried treasure.

programme.title: Worzel Gummidge Down Under
programme.desc: After joining the Crowman at an art show, Aunt Sally is taken with the riches on offer for sale of a truly great work. Worzel, also, seeks fame and fortune - and intends to prove his own artistic talents accordingly.

programme.title: Worzel Gummidge Down Under
programme.desc: Worzel and Blighty are sent away as punishment, travelling by train. After seeing members of the crew breaking for tea and cake, their attempts to get some for themselves see the train leaving the station with no staff on board!

programme.title: Worzel Gummidge Down Under
programme.desc: After joining the Crowman at an art show, Aunt Sally is taken with the riches on offer for sale of a truly great work. Worzel, also, seeks fame and fortune - and intends to prove his own artistic talents accordingly.

programme.title: Worzel Gummidge Down Under
programme.desc: A nearby convent presents the Crowman with a challenge: the commission of his first female scarecrow.

programme.title: Worzel Gummidge Down Under
programme.desc: New Zealand's governor general requires a scarecrow to prevent the eating of his beautiful lawns,so the crowman summons all of his greatest creations to assess who is best for the job.

programme.title: Happy Days: Not With My Sister You Don't
programme.desc: Richie's surprise over little sister Joanie's first real date turns to shock when he learns it's with Spike, a pint-sized replica of his Uncle Fonzie.
programme.sub-title: Not With My Sister You Don't

programme.title: Happy Days: Richie Moves Out
programme.desc: Hoping for more privacy, Richie moves out of the Cunningham home to share an apartment with his brother and soon discovers it's not the paradise he expected.

programme.title: Happy Days: You Go To My Head
programme.desc: Richie's fears get the best of him while he's reading a book on abnormal psychology and it drives him to secretly see a psychiatrist.

programme.title: Happy Days: The Cunningham Caper
programme.desc: Richie is sick in bed with the flu and is bored with eating soup and doing crossword puzzles. But when his family leaves him alone in the house, pandemonium breaks loose when Fonzie, Potsie, Ralph, and a burglar join him.

programme.title: Happy Days: The Apartment
programme.desc: Personality quirks begin to fray nerves after best friends Richie, Ralph, and Potsie rent an apartment together.

programme.title: Happy Days: Hard Cover
programme.desc: Richie and Fonzie must think fast to outwit a tough housemother after they're caught past curfew in the college girls' dormitory.

programme.title: Happy Days: Hollywood Part 2
programme.desc: Soon after getting the bad news that the studio deemed his James Dean impression obsolete, and gave Richie's wholesome good looks a contract instead, the Fonz finds a bigger fish to fry.

programme.title: Happy Days: Hollywood Part 3
programme.desc: Richie must choose either a 5-year Hollywood contract or college in Milwaukee while the Fonz accepts the California Kid's challenge to perform a dangerous water ski jump...over a shark!

programme.title: Happy Days: Fonsilectomy
programme.desc: Forced to spend the night in the hospital's children's ward, Fonzie worries more about who will be making time with his girl at the Halloween party than the tonsillectomy he's scheduled for the next morning.

programme.title: Happy Days: My Cousin the Cheat
programme.desc: Fonzie is furious with his rebellious cousin, Chachi Arcola, after Chachi cheats on a school test using answers stolen from a tutoring session with Joanie Cunningham.

programme.title: Happy Days: My Fair Fonzie
programme.desc: Snobby Cynthia Holmes schemes with her obnoxious cousin, Skippy, to make the Fonz look foolish at a country club soirée.

programme.title: Happy Days: Bye Bye Blackball
programme.desc: Richie, Ralph, and Potsie endure the harassment of "hell week" to prove themselves worthy to enter their college fraternity, Phi Kappa Nu.

programme.title: Happy Days: Richie Almost Dies
programme.desc: What begins as a humorous escapade with a motorcycle turns into a tragic vigil for the Cunninghams and their friends.

programme.title: Happy Days: Do You Want to Dance
programme.desc: Fonzie's latest romantic interest, a ballet teacher, is secretly yearning to turn professional and dance with the greats in New York. Fonzie, however, has trouble accepting that she may forgo their relationship to pursue her dreams of fame.

programme.title: Happy Days: Fearless Malph
programme.desc: When Ralph Malph is hypnotized to cure his fright after a tornado warning is posted, Ralph's loss of fear causes him to tempt fate by suggesting that the Fonz needs training wheels for his stupid motorcycle

programme.title: Happy Days: Smokin' Ain'T Cool
programme.desc: Joanie believes she has to smoke to fit in with the cool girls. She ignores the pleas from her family to stop, until the Fonz defines what cool really means

programme.title: Happy Days: Richie Gets Framed
programme.desc: Richie and the Fonz visit a massage parlor to escape the pressures of a heated class presidency race, and Richie's opposition photographs the sheet-clad candidate with a pretty masseuse.

programme.title: Police Interceptors
programme.desc: A gang of lorry thieves trigger a major manhunt.

programme.title: Police Interceptors: Episode 4
programme.desc: Documentary following police unit in Lincolnshire.

programme.title: Police Interceptors: Episode 4
programme.desc: A collection of the most extreme Interceptor chases.

programme.title: Police Interceptors: Episode 7
programme.desc: Officers clamp down on car crime at Essex's biggest shopping centre.

programme.title: Police Interceptors: Episode 4
programme.desc: Paul Jackson has his work cut out stopping a runaway driver.

programme.title: Police Interceptors: Episode 11
programme.desc: Special featuring police dogs in training and in action.

programme.title: Police Interceptors: Episode 5
programme.desc: The interceptors chase a van thief and Dan joins a manhunt.

programme.title: Police Interceptors: Episode 4
programme.desc: Dan tracks down a driver who has given him the slip.

programme.title: Police Interceptors: Episode 6
programme.desc: Spike and dog handler Ian pursue a 20-year-old drunk driver into a traveller's site.

programme.title: Police Interceptors
programme.desc: Documentary series following an Essex police unit.

programme.title: Police Interceptors
programme.desc: Documentary series following an Essex police unit.

programme.title: Police Interceptors: Episode 4
programme.desc: The Interceptors come under attack from a gang of violent, armed robbers.

programme.title: Police Interceptors: Episode 2
programme.desc: Jon Peacock has a late night encounter with two unusual lawbreakers.

programme.title: Police Interceptors: Episode 7
programme.desc: The police teams deal with an aggressive arsonist.

programme.title: Police Interceptors: Episode 8
programme.desc: Jon has a tussle with a fallen tree.

programme.title: Police Interceptors
programme.desc: Scott and Adam discover a huge stash in the boot of a car.

programme.title: Police Interceptors
programme.desc: Dan is given the runaround by an uninsured driver.

programme.title: Police Interceptors: Episode 6
programme.desc: The interceptors stop a coach load of cage fighters.

programme.title: Top 10: Secrets and Mysteries: SUPERNATURAL ABILITIES
programme.desc: Top 10 Secrets and Mysteries looks at supernatural abilities including exceptional endurance in fire-walking and the festival of penitence, telepathy, shamans, mysterious recoveries from illness, clairvoyance.

programme.title: Top 10: Secrets and Mysteries: UNEXPLAINED MESSAGES
programme.desc: Top 10 Secrets and Mysteries looks at mysterious unexplained messages from the past including the Nazca Lines, the Voynich Manuscript, the Flower of Life, Puma Punku, Leonardo Da Vinci's paintings.

programme.title: Top 10: Secrets and Mysteries: PARANORMAL ACTIVITIES
programme.desc: Top 10 Secrets and Mysteries looks at paranormal activities including apparitions and ghosts, the dark day mystery, Marian Apparitions, curses, warnings from beyond, spontaneous combustion.

programme.title: Top 10: Secrets and Mysteries: ANCIENT MEGASTRUCTURES
programme.desc: Top 10 Secrets and Mysteries looks at ancient megastructures including the Olmec Heads of Central America, Moai of Eastern Island, Petra in modern day Jordan, the Alhambra in Spain, Angkor Wat in Cambodia, Chichen Itza.

programme.title: Top 10: Secrets and Mysteries: PLACES WITH HEALING POWERS
programme.desc: Top 10 Secrets and Mysteries looks at places that are said to have healing power including Lourdes where people have been cured by drinking water from a cave, Machu Picchu, Stonehenge, the Dead Sea, the Ganges.

programme.title: Top 10: Secrets and Mysteries: MYSTERIOUS TEXTS
programme.desc: Top 10 Secrets and Mysteries looks at mysteries related to ancient texts including the Egyptian Book of the Dead, the Torah, the Secrets of Fulcanelli, the Tao Te Ching, the Vedas.

programme.title: Top 10: Secrets and Mysteries: MYSTERIES OF ANCIENT CULTURES
programme.desc: Top 10 Secrets and Mysteries looks at mysteries associated with ancient cultures including Lemuria, the Indus Valley civilization of Harappa, the Forgotten Kingdoms of Tibet, the Olmec civilization of Central America.

programme.title: Top 10: Secrets and Mysteries: RELICS OF THE SAINT
programme.desc: Top 10 Secrets and Mysteries looks at the relics of the saints including the Shrine of the Three Kings, the Tilman of Our Lady of Guadalupe, the Holy Foreskin, the Ark of the Covenant, the Remain of Mary Magdalene, Buddha's Tooth, and the Holy Grail.

programme.title: Top 10: Secrets and Mysteries: SECRET CULTS AND ORDERS
programme.desc: Top 10 Secrets and Mysteries looks at secret cults and mysterious orders including Skull and Bones at Yale University, the Freemasons, Thule and Vril Societies, the Bullingdon Club, the Hermetic Order of the Golden Dawn

programme.title: Top 10: Secrets and Mysteries: SCIENCE AND MYSTERIES FOR 21. CENTURY
programme.desc: Top 10 Secrets and Mysteries looks at some of the mysteries of modern science including memory of water, reincarnation, the beginning of life, hypnosis, the secrets of Morphic fields, time travel, why we must sleep.

programme.title: Top 10: Secrets and Mysteries: PEOPLE WITHOUT IDENTITY
programme.desc: 10 Mysterious Figures: Individuals who emerged from obscurity, made their mark on history, then vanished without a trace. Their identities remain unconfirmed, shrouding them in mystery.

programme.title: Top 10: Secrets and Mysteries: NATURE’S MAGIC
programme.desc: Natural sites worldwide hold spiritual significance for civilizations, from ancient rituals to modern reverence, each crackling with unique energy and a hint of magic.

programme.title: Top 10: Secrets and Mysteries: MYSTERIOUS WORKS OF ART
programme.desc: Great art and its creators unveil mysteries more intricate than TV dramas. Amidst theft and intrigue, we persist in solving them. Yet, the truth often lies buried with those who created it, adding layers of intrigue to the art world.

programme.title: Top 10: Secrets and Mysteries: SECRETS AND MAGIC OF ALTERNATIVE MEDICINE
programme.desc: In this episode, we dive into untraditional healing and its mysteries, challenging conventional Western medicine. Explore alternative medical miracles that will captivate you.
programme.sub-title: SECRETS AND MAGIC OF ALTERNATIVE MEDICINE

programme.title: Top 10: Secrets and Mysteries: MYSTERIOUS AND UNSOLVED DISSAPEARANCES
programme.desc: Legally declared dead, bodies never found. Disappearances spark speculation, media attention, and unanswered questions. Where did they go?

programme.title: Top 10: Secrets and Mysteries: SECRET TECHNOLOGIES
programme.desc: Scientists, engineers, military, and intelligence agencies push boundaries with unlimited budgets and secrecy. This episode unveils known (or suspected) top-secret projects.

programme.title: Top 10: Secrets and Mysteries: THE MYSTERY AND CULTS OF MUMMIES
programme.desc: Unlock the mystery of mummification from ancient Egypt to present. Explore its fascination and quest for Queen Nefertiti's mummy. Where did 70 million ancient mummies disappear? Delve into Latin American mummy legends.
programme.sub-title: THE MYSTERY AND CULTS OF MUMMIES

programme.title: Top 10: Secrets and Mysteries: UNSOLVED MYSTERIES IN PHYSICS
programme.desc: In the late 19th century, scientists thought all major physics laws were discovered. Now, we realize vast unknowns remain. Despite our understanding, mysteries persist in the physical world.

programme.title: Top 10: Secrets and Mysteries: MEDICAL MYSTERIES
programme.desc: Medical history is rife with jaw-dropping oddities, miraculous recoveries, and unsolved mysteries. Beyond shock value, these cases showcase human resilience and the vast unknowns in understanding the body and mind.

programme.title: Top 10: Secrets and Mysteries: SECRET PROJECTS AND OPERATIONS
programme.desc: Imagine a world with open use of resources from secret government projects. Little is known about these black ops. They hold mysteries and consequences, challenging democratic ideals. History reveals human experimentation, contrary to official claims.

programme.title: Top 10: Secrets and Mysteries: MYSTERIES OF HIDDEN ROOMS
programme.desc: Throughout history, humanity has been captivated by hidden places—rooms, passages, and chambers. Whether for escape or sinister motives, these ten notable hidden spaces span the ages, revealing intrigue and mystery.

programme.title: Top 10: Secrets and Mysteries: SUPRESSED INVENTIONS IN HISTORY
programme.desc: Explore lost or suppressed inventions from history, some skeptical, others mysterious. Despite doubts, many are useful today. Stay open-minded as we delve into these intriguing relics.

programme.title: Top 10: Secrets and Mysteries: HUMAN SACRIFICES
programme.desc: Human sacrifice, practiced since the Paleolithic era, was seen as a gift to gods for cosmic order. Found in black magic, voodoo, and Satanism, these rituals may hint at a mysterious connection to higher forces.

programme.desc: Legend Hunters try to determine if a eight foot tall ape runs wild in North America.

programme.title: Legend Hunters: Ghost Hunters

programme.title: Legend Hunters: Hunters of Noah's Ark
programme.desc: The story of Noah‚Äôs Ark is one of the most enduring tales of the Old Testament. Thought to rest in Turkey, discovery of the Ark has been the focus of many explorers throughout the ages.

programme.title: Museum Secrets: Inside the Louvre

programme.desc: From the mystery of a Pharaoh’s gold to the strange practice of animal mummification, discover the secrets inside the Egyptian Museum.

programme.title: Greatest Crimes of All Time: Female Murderers
programme.desc: Women usually kill in their own peculiar way: sophisticated, tactically, and from behind. They prefer tainted medicines, knives, or poison. And they often kill for years, unnoticed and in secret.

programme.title: Greatest Crimes of All Time: Hitmen
programme.desc: Hitmen are masters of covering their tracks. Their only concern is for loyalty and the greatest possible profit. Investigators must always take care not to become the next victim.

programme.title: Greatest Crimes of All Time: Ruthless Killers
programme.desc: Ruthless Killers: They kill for all manner of reasons. Their victims: men, women, and even children. What motivates them, and how can they be stopped? Investigators reach their limits hunting them down.

programme.title: Greatest Crimes of All Time: Hostage-Takers

programme.title: Greatest Crimes of All Time: Woman Killers

programme.title: Greatest Crimes of All Time: Gang Bosses
programme.desc: They rule the underworld: gang bosses. They make millions with racketeering and drug trafficking. For investigators, it is more than a fight against criminality – it is a veritable war.

programme.title: Greatest Crimes of All Time: Angels of Death
programme.desc: In hospitals and nursing wards, death is an everyday occurrence. And that is precisely what they count on: angels of death. Some of them rack up more victims in this way than the worst serial killers in the world.

programme.title: Greatest Crimes of All Time: Serial Killers
programme.desc: Serial Killers victims are often selected at random. The murders have only one thing in common: the bodies are savagely disfigured. Hunting them leads investigators into a world of darkness.

programme.title: Museum Secrets: Inside the National Museum of Anthropology, Mexico City

programme.desc: Police officer Amy Johnson has been happily married for four years when, in the blink of an eye, her husband becomes a different man.

programme.title: Stalked: Someone's Watching: Dangerous Games
programme.desc: At first, Karen Welch thinks nothing of the mysterious call she receives on Christmas Eve.

programme.title: Stalked: Someone's Watching: Teenage Obsession
programme.desc: When outgoing Cameron Wallace meets shy Ryan Clutter in a high school art class, they are only acquaintances. Little does she know that Ryan will stalk her for the next ten years.

programme.title: Stalked: Someone's Watching: Dire Straits
programme.desc: A college student in Staten Island meets a deceivingly charming man who sweeps her off her feet. But the relationship turns sour when his true nature is revealed.

programme.title: Cold Blood: Island of Lies
programme.desc: An investigation into a 23-year-old Hawaiian tourist's rape and murder reveals a world of drugs and deception few vacationers ever see. And it's up to one lone detective to get the victims family some justice.

programme.title: Cold Blood: Somebody Knows
programme.desc: Kimberly Dunkin’s body is found with a single gunshot to the head, and though investigators suspect she was the victim of a gang war, it will take 15 years, a deck of playing cards, and a lucky break before the case finally begins to unravel.

programme.title: Cold Blood: California Girl Gone
programme.desc: On her last day as a high school freshman, a 14-year-old girl is brutally beaten and set on fire. The horrifying crime sends shockwaves through her California community and with a possible serial killer on the loose, investigators hunt for a monster.

programme.title: Cold Blood: Who Killed Daddy’s Little Girl?
programme.desc: When a pregnant 23-year-old Iowa woman is shot in her own bed, investigators must quickly determine if they are looking for someone with a deadly grudge against the couple or a more sinister culprit closer to home.

programme.title: Cold Blood: Screams of Silence
programme.desc: While building their seaside dream home in Connecticut, Matthew Monahon discovers his pregnant wife Kelsey hogtied on the floor and clinging to life. And when she dies days later, a $300,000 life insurance policy casts him as the number one suspect.

programme.title: Cold Blood: Mommy Speak For Me
programme.desc: In Cleveland, a 14-year-old African-American girl is snatched off the street, assaulted and murdered on her way to school. Initially, detectives work the case 24/7. But it will take a lucky break and nearly 30 years before the culprit is behind bars.

programme.title: Cold Blood: An Angel Taken
programme.desc: A devout Mormon and popular University student, 19-year-old Brooke Wilberger is abducted from an Oregon parking lot in broad daylight. Police immediately launch a massive search and the story of the missing 19-year-old soon captures headlines nationwide.

programme.title: Cold Blood: Sheltered From The Truth
programme.desc: It’s a September morning in 1973, and Debra Dill’s bludgeoned body is discovered next to her car in rural Maine. Her grieving community wakes up to a nightmare, but it will take nearly two decades before the killer is behind bars.

programme.title: Dark Waters of Crime: Devil in the Woods
programme.desc: On June 24th 1990, the day after a rock festival near Roseisle in Manitoba, 16 year-old Brigitte Grenier is found lying dead in a stream in the middle of the woods. The teenager’s body shows signs of violence and sexual mutilations.

programme.title: Dark Waters of Crime: Scenario for a Murder
programme.desc: Since October 10th 2008, no-one has seen friendly 38 year-old bachelor John Altinger. All that his friends know is that he was going to a garage in a residential area of Edmonton, to meet a woman he’d met on an online dating site.

programme.title: Naked Science: Making Mountains
programme.desc: The Rockies. Magnificent and awe-inspiring mysterious, the Rockies are one of the longest mountain ranges in the world. Naked Science investigates the geological processes that build these massive mountains.

programme.title: Naked Science: Diamonds
programme.desc: Naked Science follows the story of a diamond from the creation of carbon inside the heart of a dying sun, through to the cutting of a diamond mine in South Africa.

programme.title: Naked Science: Anatomy Of Tornado
programme.desc: Tornados are one of the most destructive forces of the planets with winds at 300 miles an hour. They can destroy buildings and hurl cars hundreds of feet through the air.

programme.title: Naked Science: Anatomy Of A Hurricane
programme.desc: Hurricanes are one of the most destructive weather events to strike our planet and have the same power as a nuclear weapon.

programme.title: Naked Science: Saturn
programme.desc: Saturn is the second-largest planet in the solar system. Storms on this gas giant can cover an area bigger than the United States Clouds of the gas race around the planet at over 1000 miles per hour generating fierce electrical storms and lightning.

programme.title: Naked Science: Death Of The Earth
programme.desc: The earth is 4.5 billion years old and it is slowly dying. Tectonic activity is slowly cooling the planet – the continents will eventually move together to form a new supercontinent called Pangea Ultima.

programme.title: Naked Science: Magnetic Earth
programme.desc: The Earth hurtles through space protected by an invisible force field, known as a magnetosphere.

programme.title: Naked Science: How To Destroy A Planet
programme.desc: The Earth is built to last. It is a 4,550,000,000-year-old, 5,973,600,000,000,000,000,000-tonne ball of rock and iron. It has taken more devastating asteroid hits in its lifetime than you've had hot dinners, and it still orbits happily around the sun.

programme.desc: Captain James T Kirk’s battle with the Gorn alien is one of the most popular episodes from the original series.

programme.desc: Who is the greatest international action hero of all time, Terry Schappert?

programme.title: Hollywood Weapons: We Hear Ya
programme.desc: Larry and Terry test several requests from viewers.

programme.title: Bang Goes The Theory
programme.desc: The science of IVF; following couples who undertake IVF treatment; going 360 degrees on a swing.

programme.title: Bang Goes The Theory
programme.desc: The origins of weather; seasons allow life to form; Jem checks out wave power in Scotland.

programme.title: Bang Goes The Theory
programme.desc: Liz visits the volcano that stopped air travel in 2010; predicting probabilities in Las Vegas.

programme.title: Bang Goes The Theory
programme.desc: Jem builds a laser; Yan recreates a demonstration to calculate the speed of light.

programme.title: Bang Goes The Theory
programme.desc: Boat made out of ice; comets; science behind hypothermia.

programme.title: Bang Goes The Theory
programme.desc: The future of recycling; how GPS works and how it is changing the world; using science to crack a magic trick.

programme.title: Destruction Decoded: CONSTRUCTION
programme.desc: Bridges and tunnels have transformed the way we live, making the inaccessible easy to reach, and turning time consuming journeys into convenient commutes, but when things go wrong they can go very wrong indeed.

programme.title: Inside Outer Space: Episode 1: The Solar System
programme.desc: This is a series packed with space stories and information about our universe covering what we can see, how we can live in space and what science tells us about the past and the future as we journey inside out of space.

programme.title: Moon Shots: Inside the Lost Apollo Archive
programme.desc: The world famous Apollo missions are some of the greatest feats ever accomplished by man. With rare archive footage and unprecedented access to life on board the Apollo spacecrafts, this stunning one-hour special brings scientific history to life.

programme.title: Neil Armstrong: One Small Step
programme.desc: Neil Armstrong has stamped his place in history by becoming the first person to walk on the moon.

programme.title: The Apollo Experience: Apollo 17: Part 1
programme.desc: Between 1960 and 1972, mankind boldly went where no human had been before. Across 11 manned missions, 24 men flew to the moon and 12 walked upon its surface. This film documents Apollo 17 through the eyes of the astronauts who flew.

programme.title: The Apollo Experience: Apollo 17: Part 2
programme.desc: In this episode, hear first hand testimonials and dialogue from the incredible men who walked on the moon. Incredible footage and photos give a unique and beautiful experience of their data collection process and journey home.

programme.title: The Fabric of the Cosmos: Quantum Leap
programme.desc: Join Brian Greene on a wild ride into the weird realm of quantum physics, which governs the universe on the tiniest of scales.

programme.title: The Fabric of the Cosmos: What Is Space?
programme.desc: Brian Greene reveals space as a dynamic fabric that can stretch, twist, warp, and ripple under the influence of gravity, and explores dark energy, which makes up 70% of the universe.

programme.title: The Fabric of the Cosmos: Universe or Multiverse?
programme.desc: Cutting-edge theories are suggesting that our universe may not be the only universe. Instead, it may be just one of an infinite number of worlds that make up the multiverse.

programme.title: The Fabric of the Cosmos: The Illusion of Time

programme.title: EARTH´S EVIL TWIN

programme.title: The New Frontier: Return To The Moon

programme.title: The New Frontier: Exo Planeta
programme.desc: As scientists and astronomers peer closer at nearby stars with ever more fidelity they are discovering planets; exo planets, worlds outside our own solar system.

programme.title: The New Frontier: Heavy Lift

programme.title: The New Frontier: Slingshot

programme.title: The New Frontier: From There To Here
programme.desc: Series introduction and the study of Earth from orbit.

programme.title: The New Frontier: Mars: Adapt Or Leave
programme.desc: As the latest successes and failures come and go Mars continues to give up her secrets.

programme.title: The New Frontier: PEERING INTO THE ABYSS

programme.title: The New Frontier: The Inner Worlds

programme.title: The New Frontier: Invasion on Mars
programme.desc: The EXO Mars and Curiosity continue their scientific discoveries as we prepare new missions both robotic and manned to the Red planet Mars.

programme.title: The New Frontier: THE NEXT BIG LEAPS
programme.desc: Projects in the works. SunRISE, SPHEREx, NEOS and others, their mission to expand our knowledge of Earth, the surrounding space, Dark Matter, and the earliest galaxies. N

programme.title: The New Frontier: Fresh Eyes
programme.desc: Two new space telescopes are in orbit and completing their shakedown; Gaia and TESS they too will be joined by the James Webb Telescope.

programme.title: RISING TIDES
programme.desc: The next generation of Earth observation satellites are now in place maintaining the continuity of long-term climate data, in particular the planets rising temperatures and related ocean levels.

programme.title: The New Frontier: Mercury Or Bust

programme.title: The New Detectives: Camera Clues
programme.desc: Forensic Photography: Forensic photographers are among the first people at a crime scene, capturing vital clues on film. What do the cameras capture that can't be seen first-hand, and who are the men and women who analyze the camera's clues?

programme.title: The New Detectives: Random Targets
programme.desc: Most victims of multiple murderers are meticulously chosen because of a mutual connection with the killer or because they match an intricate set of criteria that fits the killer's MO.

programme.title: The New Detectives: Scent Of The Kill
programme.desc: A dog can be a dead man's best friend. Dogs have been trained to sniff out corpses, drugs, explosives, and missing persons. They're often the first to find the essential clue that sets an investigation in motion.

programme.title: The New Detectives: Coroner's Casebook
programme.desc: A good coroner provides what's necessary to solve a crime. A bad one can spoil an otherwise rock-solid case. Cyril Wecht and Henry Lee, two of the country's most respected coroners, share their cases and insights into crime solving.

programme.title: The New Detectives: Mind Hunters
programme.desc: Psychological Profiling: Journey into the dark recesses and calculated madness present only in our worst nightmares, and in the minds of serial killers.

programme.title: The New Detectives: A Federal Offense
programme.desc: When there's a difficult case to crack  whether it involves drugs, arson, or weapons  the investigators and scientists of the Bureau of Alcohol, Tobacco and Firearms have the means to crack it.

programme.title: The New Detectives: Dead Men Do Talk
programme.desc: Profiles the work of world-renowned forensic experts and the procedures they use to solve murders and other mysteries in minutes or centuries after they happen.

programme.title: The New Detectives: Face Of Tragedy
programme.desc: Forensic Sculpting: Forensic sculptors retrieve people from oblivion. Using clay and an intricate knowledge of anatomy, forensic arts place a face on an unidentified skull, recreating the victim's likeness, which often leads to his name.

programme.title: The New Detectives: Toxic Death
programme.desc: Sometimes, the cause of death does not match the scene of the crime. When an untraceable poison is used to commit murder, homicide detectives turn to forensic toxicologists to follow a killer's tracks and expose a toxic death.

programme.title: The New Detectives: Mind Hunters
programme.desc: Psychological Profiling: Journey into the dark recesses and calculated madness present only in our worst nightmares, and in the minds of serial killers.

programme.title: The New Detectives: Signed In Blood
programme.desc: Handwriting Analysis: We've all heard that our handwriting tells more about our personalities than we think. Are we risk-takers, have low-esteem, fun loving, or are we capable of murder?

programme.title: The New Detectives: At Close Range
programme.desc: When a victim is gunned down at point-blank range, police often assume that a friend or acquaintance is to blame.

programme.title: The New Detectives: True Crime
programme.desc: They know as much about crime as any crime fighter, or any criminal. They're the crime writers, and through their eyes we see murder most foul.

programme.title: The New Detectives: Fatal Impressions
programme.desc: 911 receives a desperate call in Fort Worth, Texas. A man's wife is shot. Forensic investigators search for clues in unlikely places, hoping the victim herself could provide information needed to determine how and why she died.

programme.title: The New Detectives: Written In Blood
programme.desc: In North Carolina, the home of a prominent couple becomes an unlikely scene of terrible bloodshed. Across the country, a California woman vanishes, worrying her family and the investigators trying to find her.

programme.title: The New Detectives: Loved To Death
programme.desc: A victim usually never foresees danger when the perpetrator turns out to be a friend or a lover. Science and microscopic evidence can unmask these killers and find justice for those who are Loved To Death.

programme.title: The New Detectives: Written In Bone
programme.desc: At the scene of a murder, sometimes the victim provides the only clues to their killer. Forensic anthropologists use skeletal remains to decipher the clues written in the bones.

programme.title: Sensing Murder: Taken Out
