programme.title: Sister, Sister: Halloween
programme.desc: While the twins go in search of a Halloween rave, Lisa and Ray attend a fancy party thrown by a prospective client.

programme.title: Sister, Sister: Weird Science
programme.desc: Tia and Tamera think they're in for a "free ride" when Ray starts dating their science teacher.

programme.title: Sister, Sister: Thanksgiving in Hawaii (Part I)
programme.desc: When Ray and Lisa take the girls to Hawaii for Thanksgiving, they're forced to share their condo with their "exes," who have begun dating each other.

programme.title: Sister, Sister: Thanksgiving in Hawaii (Part II)
programme.desc: While Ray and Lisa frantically search for the twins, who are missing in Hawaii, Roger opens the Campbell/Landry home to hobos for Thanksgiving dinner.

programme.title: Sister, Sister: Private School
programme.desc: The prospect of Tia's attending a prestigious prep school puts social pressure on Tia and financial pressure on Lisa.

programme.title: Sister, Sister: Christmas
programme.desc: When a loan shark comes after Ray's father for a bad debt, the family comes to his rescue.

programme.title: Sister, Sister: Double Double Date
programme.desc: The twins find themselves in double trouble when they switch identities and Tia winds up with two dates for the same party.

programme.title: Sister, Sister: Reality Really Bites
programme.desc: For her film class, Tia "spices up" her family documentary by throwing all sorts of monkey wrenches into their lives.

programme.title: Sister, Sister: The Volunteers
programme.desc: While volunteering at a local retirement home, Tamera becomes determined to help a grumpy old man find happiness again.

programme.title: Sister, Sister: Valentines Day
programme.desc: Valentine's Day finds Tamera trying to "steal back" her hot date, and Ray and Lisa wondering if the other person is in love with them.

programme.title: Sister, Sister: Paper or Plastic?
programme.desc: Tamera crosses the picket lines to work at a supermarket, but soon realizes that being a "scab" isn't all it's cracked up to be.

programme.title: Sister, Sister: The Piano Lesson
programme.desc: Ray learns a "noteworthy" lesson in honesty when he "fibs" and tells a tone-deaf Tamera that she plays the piano well, and she winds up humiliating herself at a piano recital.

programme.title: Sister, Sister: Summer Bummer
programme.desc: Tia and Tamera trade places so that Tia can be with a cute guy in summer school and Tamera can "goof off" by taking Tia's spot as a camp counselor.

programme.title: Sister, Sister: The Candidate
programme.desc: Tamera learns how much she needs Tia's help when she runs for class president.

programme.title: Sister, Sister: Big Twin on Campus
programme.desc: When Tia poses as a college student to impress her older boyfriend, Tamera goes to great lengths to fit in with Tia's new circle of "mature" friends.

programme.title: Sister, Sister: The Audition
programme.desc: Tamera's budding career as a lowdown blues singer is threatened when she gets "too happy" after her dream date asks her out!

programme.title: Sister, Sister: When a Man Loves Two Women
programme.desc: Unbeknownst to the twins, they've both got a crush on the same guy: Roger!
programme.sub-title: When a Man Loves Two Women

programme.title: Sister, Sister: You Are So Beautiful
programme.desc: Curiosity about their birth parents leads the twins, Ray, and Lisa on a quest to learn more about the girls' past.

programme.title: Sister, Sister: Gimme a Brake
programme.desc: Tempted by the freedom of a new car, Tia and Tamera soon find themselves on a collision course with disaster when their car drops off a dock and into Lake Erie!

programme.title: Sister, Sister: Daddys Girl
programme.desc: Tamera learns she's never too old to be a daddy's girl when she finds herself jealous of the growing closeness between Ray and Tia.

programme.title: Sister, Sister: Sis Boom Bah
programme.desc: In a bid to boost their popularity at school, the twins try out for the cheerleading squad -- and Ray tries his hand at coaching them.

programme.title: Sister, Sister: Kid-Napped
programme.desc: When a new girl in school threatens Tia and Tamera's popularity, the twins join forces to win back their friends with the help of a music superstar.

programme.title: Sister, Sister: Boy From the Hood
programme.desc: Tamera falls for Tia's childhood friend Darnell, who's all grown up and running with a fast crowd.

programme.title: Sister, Sister: I'll be There
programme.desc: Tia takes a job in a '50s diner to get away from Tamera and is shocked when Tamera starts working there, too.

programme.title: Sister, Sister: Ch-Ch-Ch-Changes
programme.desc: The twins are thrilled when Ray and Lisa decide to take their relationship to a more romantic level, but have second thoughts when Roger outlines the downsides of their parents dating.

programme.title: Sister, Sister: Double Exposure
programme.desc: When Tia poses for Tamera's class picture, the gorgeous photo that results makes Tamera extremely popular, and Tia extremely resentful.

programme.title: Sister, Sister: Some Like it Hockey
programme.desc: Determined to make the school's all-male hockey team, Tia and Tamera must disguise themselves as boys and pretend they're just two of the guys!

programme.title: Sister, Sister: Bring on Debate
programme.desc: Ray feels left out by the close bond Tamera forms with her new music teacher.

programme.title: Sister, Sister: Little Man Date
programme.desc: Tia and her new beau Kyle decide to fix up Tamera with Kyle's little brother – who turns out to be a lot littler than anyone thought!

programme.title: Sister, Sister: Ski Squad
programme.desc: A ski trip designed to give the twins some independence from their parents has them longing for the comforts of home after they're trapped in an avalanche.

programme.title: Sister, Sister: Cafeteria Lady
programme.desc: Lisa gets a new job in the high school cafeteria, but her outrageous behavior proves to be a major embarrassment to Tia.

programme.title: Sister, Sister: Three the Heart Way
programme.desc: Dateless Tamera feels like a third wheel when Tia and Roger get matched up on Valentine's Day.

programme.title: Sister, Sister: Model Tia
programme.desc: The family flashes back to the Disco Decade when Ray and Lisa attend a 1970s theme party, while Tamera falls prey to a man who promises to launch her modeling career.

programme.title: Sister, Sister: My Guy
programme.desc: A high school nerd out to woo Tia turns to Tamera for help in updating his image, but when his makeover is complete, Tamera realizes she wants to keep him for herself!

programme.title: Sister, Sister: Double Dutch
programme.desc: When a girl from Tia's old neighborhood challenges her to a double Dutch jump rope contest, Tia becomes obsessed with winning.

programme.title: Sister, Sister: Inherit the Twin
programme.desc: Tia and Tamera wind up in student court, where the two boys who are supposedly defending them wind up dissing them instead.

programme.title: Sister, Sister: Slime Party
programme.desc: Tia and Tamera audition for a game show, where their opponents are famous TV twins Mary-Kate and Ashley Olsen.

programme.title: Sister, Sister: Guardian Angel
programme.desc: The spirit of Ray's late wife returns to help Tamera make a tough decision, and Ray to get on with his life.

programme.title: Flashpoint
programme.desc: The SRU's attempt to take down a crime boss, but when the plan is botched, the boss flees, taking his girlfriend, a criminal Informant, with him.

programme.title: Flashpoint
programme.desc: An outsider is brought in to perform psychological testing on Team One, but fearing someone will be let go, they start to turn on each other. Meanwhile, Ed is shot on the way to the hospital for the birth of his child.

programme.title: Flashpoint
programme.desc: Team One races to track down Ed's shooter, while his wife has problems delivering their baby.

programme.title: Flashpoint
programme.desc: Team One confronts a rioting crowd after a cop who mistakenly shot and killed a young boy is declared innocent.

programme.title: Flashpoint
programme.desc: Team One goes up against a notorious serial robber who streams his robberies on the internet.

programme.title: Flashpoint
programme.desc: The SRU is called in when a mother and her daughter are abducted.

programme.title: Flashpoint
programme.desc: Team One struggles with a secret that could compromise their drug bust and end Wordy's career.

programme.title: Flashpoint
programme.desc: When Raf, a new recruit, joins Team One, he learns what it means to be a member of the Strategic Response Unit on one of their toughest days of the year forhostage and suicide situations.

programme.title: Flashpoint
programme.desc: Two bombs are set off while Sam, Spike and Raff are responding to a Hot Call at an office building, trapping them and nine civilians inside with a third seemingly non-diffusible bomb.

programme.title: Flashpoint
programme.desc: Team One has an unusual case when a passenger jet is hijacked by a group demanding the release of a prisoner.

programme.title: Flashpoint
programme.desc: Team One responds to 911 call where a masked gunman threatens a group of teens and kidnaps one of them.

programme.title: Flashpoint
programme.desc: Team One thinks they have a simple case of robbery on their hands,but things get complicated when the victim sabotages the rescue.

programme.title: Flashpoint
programme.desc: Team One is alerted to a situation in a bank where a woman who appears under duress is withdrawing all her money. When she sabotages her own rescue and escapes with her tormentor, the team turn their attention to her family, who is also in danger.

programme.title: Flashpoint
programme.desc: As Donna gets ready to walk down the aisle, things changed drastically. She learns some surprising news regarding a mentor she once worked with.

programme.title: Flashpoint
programme.desc: Team One must figure out how to get a Chinatown gang to release a local shop owner, then they also have to figure out how to get the one who shot the owner's daughter free as well.

programme.title: Flashpoint
programme.desc: An ex-cop with a grudge against Greg Parker takes him hostage and threatens to drop him off an arena's megatron if Greg can't talk him down. Meanwhile his team discovers that they are helpless to use tactical options to save their boss.

programme.title: Flashpoint
programme.desc: When Spike answers Sam's sister's desperate plea for help, he is forced, at gunpoint, to help two criminals steal evidence from the fortress that is the Metro Evidence Depot.

programme.title: Flashpoint
programme.desc: Team One is called in to try and stop a conflict at a psychiatric hospital where a stabbing has just taken place.

programme.title: Flashpoint
programme.desc: Greg Parker immediately faces suspension from the team after keeping the relationship between two team members a secret.

programme.title: Flashpoint
programme.desc: A fire captain who feels nothing he does is good enough is given the chance to prove to himself and to an injured member of his crew that he can set things right.

programme.title: Teletubbies
programme.desc: The Teletubbies want to go to sleep, but Tinky Winky finds Po already asleep in his bed. So he goes to look for somewhere else to sleep.

programme.title: Teletubbies
programme.desc: The Teletubbies watch Andy Brown and some children looking for Grasshoppers. In Teletubbyland the Teletubbies are jumping over different things.

programme.title: Teletubbies
programme.desc: The Teletubbies watch some children perform a Maori Song. In Teletubbyland, Po sings a lovely song. Gradually the other Teletubbies join in, until they sing a lovely song together. The Teletubbies love to sing.

programme.title: Teletubbies
programme.desc: Laa-Laa comes through to door to say ‘eh-oh’, she joins the others to watch some children squeezing things. A squeezy tube appears in Teletubbyland.

programme.title: Teletubbies
programme.desc: The Teletubbies watch some children learn about the number three. Tinky Winky, Dipsy and Laa-Laa go for a walk. They count three trees and three flowers and Po follows behind counting too.

programme.title: Teletubbies
programme.desc: In Teletubbyland, it's time for Tubby Toast, but there's no Tubby Toast for Laa‑Laa. She is very sad, so the other Tubbies share their extra toast with Laa-Laa. Some children learn about the number six.

programme.title: Teletubbies
programme.desc: Teletubbies love to play peek-a-boo. Then the Teletubbies and a group of children see Tamzin Griffin pretend to be Funny Lady when she tells the story of the Naughty Sock. Dipsy can't sleep when the other Teletubbies are too noisy.

programme.title: Teletubbies
programme.desc: Some children play a game called oranges and lemons. A bell appears in Teletubbyland and makes a lovely ringing noise. The noise is so loud it makes the Tubbies fall over laughing

programme.title: Teletubbies
programme.desc: Bryony loves to play the violin. She talks about her violin and they plays 'Twinkle Twinkle Little Star'. The Teletubbies sit down to listen to some lovely music, but the Noo Noo is making too much noise.

programme.title: Teletubbies
programme.desc: Tinky Winky & Po burst out of the house to say Eh-oh and enjoy a big hug. Then they do it again. They join Dipsy & Laa-Laa to watch a little girl read the story of Jack & The Beanstalk.

programme.title: Teletubbies
programme.desc: Po fetches the others to watch some girls perform an Indian dance. In Teletubbyland, Dipsy dances a very special dance. Dipsy showed Tinky Winky his very special dance.

programme.title: Teletubbies
programme.desc: The Teletubbies see a little girl and her brother who love salad. They go shopping in a market a market with their grandma to buy some salad to eat.

programme.title: Teletubbies
programme.desc: Dipsy is out for a walk when a strong wind blows his hat away. The hat disappears into Home Hill, but Dipsy doesn't know where it has gone. Noo Noo blows the hat back out of Home Hill to Dipsy, who is very happy.

programme.title: Teletubbies
programme.desc: The Teletubbies watch a little boy help his mum paint some Rangoli patterns for the festival of Diwali. A decoration appears in Teletubbyland.

programme.title: Teletubbies
programme.desc: Dipsy & Laa-Laa hug and dance and then join the others to watch some children and Andy Brown play catching leaves in Autumn. T

programme.title: Teletubbies
programme.desc: Po jumps out from behind Tinky Winky and they run off to watch Andy Brown and some children looking for creatures. They find some woodlice under a log.

programme.title: Teletubbies
programme.desc: The Teletubbies watch a little girl making breakfast for her mum. Laa-Laa and Po wake up and play a great game saying ‘Good Morning’ to each other.

programme.title: Teletubbies
programme.desc: The Teletubbies watch Oliver and his Llama. Po is feeling happy. She jumps and skips over Teletubbyland singing to herself.

programme.title: Teletubbies
programme.desc: Tinky Winky spills his Tubby Custard and the Noo-noo tidies it up. Tinky Winky joins the others to watch a little girl paint a Turkey using her hand as a template.

programme.title: Teletubbies
programme.desc: The Teletubbies watch a little boy and older friend playing a tune on a piano. In Teletubbyland, a piano appears. Tinky Winky and Po both have great fun bashing away at the keys, but when they play nicely, they play a lovely tune.

programme.title: Teletubbies
programme.desc: The Teletubbies watch a Spanish girl play her violin for her friends. In Teletubbyland the Teletubbies watch Po dance.

programme.title: Teletubbies
programme.desc: The Teletubbies are feeling tired. Andy Brown and a little girl look for spiders in a garden. Two voice trumpets rise and tell Tinky Winky the nursery rhyme 'Incy Wincy Spider'.

programme.title: Teletubbies
programme.desc: The Teletubbies and a group of children see Tamzin Griffin pretend to be Funny Lady and tell the story of the naughty snake.

programme.title: Teletubbies
programme.desc: The Teletubbies watch some children making marks. They use paint and different objects to make a colourful picture.

programme.title: Teletubbies
programme.desc: A little boy goes for a ride on his Grandad’s motorbike and sidecar. In Teletubbyland, Po went to see Tinky Winky on her scooter.

programme.title: Teletubbies
programme.desc: The Teletubbies watch Andy Brown and some children visit some cygnets. In Teletubbyland, it's time for the Tubbies to go to bed, but Po is not tired, so she rides her scooter.

programme.title: Teletubbies
programme.desc: Two girls make a picture of a gold fish pond using tissue paper. Dipsy sees a cloud and decides to follow it. The cloud rains and leaves a puddle.

programme.title: Teletubbies
programme.desc: A voice trumpet appears and teaches the Teletubbies a winter nursery rhyme. Po’s present is a little frosty cloud that begins to snow.

programme.title: Teletubbies
programme.desc: The Noo-noo wakes up the Teletubbies to watch some children paint a huge mural using spray paints. Laa-Laa is making Tubby Custard, but the Tubby Custard machine makes a big Tubby Custard squirt on Tinky Winky, Dipsy, Po and Laa-Laa.

programme.title: Teletubbies
programme.desc: The Teletubbies watch a girl talking all about her Dad's lorry. Dipsy goes for a long walk, but the other Teletubbies decide not to join him.

programme.title: Teletubbies
programme.desc: The Teletubbies finish up their toast in time to see Alex Pascall and some children go for a walk and sing Good Morning to everyone they meet. All the Teletubbies wanted to play with Dipsy, but Dipsy wants to dance.

programme.title: Teletubbies
programme.desc: The Teletubbies watch some children playing with torches. In Teletubbyland, Po chases Tinky Winky, and Po catches him.

programme.title: Teletubbies
programme.desc: A voice trumpet brings a magical, musical message for Po. The Teletubbies watch two children whose father is a lighthouse keeper, and learn that lighthouses have lots of steps.

programme.title: Teletubbies
programme.desc: Andy Brown and some children visit and aquarium to learn how things swim in the water. The Teletubbies play an in and out game when a cloud appears in Teletubbyland.

programme.title: Teletubbies
programme.desc: A voice trumpet rises and Laa-Laa and Dipsy play a going up and going down game. They go up the hill and then they go down the hill. They do it again and again.

programme.title: Teletubbies
programme.desc: Po feels like having some Tubby Custard. The Teletubbies watch some children visiting a conservatory and drawing cacti. Tinky Winky goes for a walk. Five birds appear in Teletubbyland and the Teletubbies dance round the flowers.

programme.title: Teletubbies
programme.desc: The Teletubbies watch Brian Cant read a story to some children. In Teletubbyland, somebody spilled the Tubby Custard, made a mess with their Tubby Toast at the control panel, and fell fast asleep.

programme.title: Bob the Builder
programme.desc: Bob is building a new swimming pool for the Mayor. He asks Muck to collect some special tiles from JJ, explaining that they are very fragile and easy to break.

programme.title: Bob the Builder
programme.desc: Bob and the gang are replacing some old pipes outside the town hall. Molly, meanwhile, is practicing for her first aid exam with a reluctant JJ as guinea pig. When Wendy calls JJ’s yard to order a special pipe and Molly offers to deliver it.

programme.title: Bob the Builder
programme.desc: Bob is replacing a potting shed on Mr Beasley’s allotment. In the old shed is a chest which Mr Beasley doesn’t need any more. Meanwhile Wendy calls at JJ’s for the materials for the new shed.

programme.title: Bob the Builder
programme.desc: A new machine has come to the Valley – Packer the Delivery Truck. He’s to work with Farmer Pickles team. Farmer Pickles gives Packer his jobs and he sets off on his first delivery round.

programme.title: Bob the Builder
programme.desc: Trix is looking for something to do so JJ sends her to deliver a bag of wood chippings to Bob who is building a Jungle Jim climbing frame in the school playground. Trix likes being busy and starts offering to fetch and carry everything for everyone.

programme.title: Bob the Builder
programme.desc: The team are building a bunkhouse for Farmer Pickles guests The Pickers! Micky, Vicky and Little Ricky are helping with the sunflower harvest. The cabin is to be made of logs so Lofty’s job is very important today, lifting and laying the logs.

programme.title: Bob the Builder
programme.desc: Bob has a plan to accommodate all of the newcomers. He’s designed a kit house that clips together quickly and easily. Bob’s dad Robert comes to the valley to help build the houses, while Mr and Mrs Bentley conduct a survey on the newcomers’ requirements.

programme.title: Bob the Builder
programme.desc: Whilst Bob and the team build Annie Pickles new seafront house, Muck is worried that Scoop is a better digger than he is and decides to prove himself with a sandcastle competition.

programme.title: Bob the Builder
programme.desc: Bob is putting up some new signs in Sunflower Valley to help people find their way round the valley. Meanwhile Dizzy has been asked by Ela Stevenson if she’ll be the ‘walking bus’ and help the children get to school.

programme.title: Bob the Builder
programme.desc: Bob is tiling a roof and calls at JJ’s yard to pick up the materials. But there is only one pallet of the requisite red tiles in stock, so JJ promises to send the remainder of the order as soon as his delivery arrives.

programme.title: Bob the Builder
programme.desc: Bob has a day off and he takes a trip to Farmer Pickles farm to take some photographs for the town photography competition. Wendy, meanwhile, has been called to the school hall where a pipe has burst, threatening the competition.

programme.title: Trains and Treehouses
programme.desc: Bob and his ‘Can-Do’ crew are back on site with a brand new adventure! Join Bob as he builds a train set for Mr Bentley as a birthday surprise and helps find a family of squirrels a new tree-house home.

programme.title: Bob the Builder
programme.desc: Bob and the team are finishing refurbishing the old watermill ready to begin milling the first Sunflower Valley flour. Work is just about to start when Dizzy hears a squeaking noise coming from above. There are bats in the old mill!

programme.title: Bob the Builder
programme.desc: Now that Robert has taken over at the old yard, Wendy can finally move to Sunflower Valley! Bob is still in Bobsville, while the machines are in charge of setting up Wendy’s caravan, complete with solar panels, as a special surprise.

programme.title: Bob the Builder
programme.desc: Bob’s renovating a dilapidated old watermill in Sunflower Valley – it’s going to make flour from Farmer Pickles sunflower farm. After checking that it’s safe, Bob realises that one of the millstones has gone missing. How very odd!

programme.title: When Bob Became a Builder
programme.desc: It’s a beautiful day in Sunflower Valley and Bob and his machine team are building a new picnic area at the beach. Dizzy, Lofty and Scrambler are clearing stuff that’s been washed up on the beach, while Benny has a great time digging with Scoop and Muck.

programme.title: Bob the Builder
programme.desc: Bob is rendering a wall for Mr Sabatini. The job is straightforward, but the finished result looks rather plain, so Bob offers to get some tiles from JJ’s yard to decorate it. While Bob is gone, Mr Sabatini demonstrates his soccer skills to Dizzy.

programme.title: Bob the Builder
programme.desc: Bob and the gang have been asked to replace some burst pipes under the main road. Meanwhile Jenny is in town to persuade Wendy to take a much-needed holiday. Unfortunately, there’s lots of work to do and Wendy simply doesn’t have time to go away.

programme.title: Bob the Builder
programme.desc: Bob and Wendy are converting an old shop into a brand new restaurant for Mr Sabatini. Mr Sabatini asks Wendy to be the guest of honour at the grand opening. She is delighted but terrified at the prospect of giving a speech in public.

programme.title: Bob the Builder
programme.desc: Bob and the team are drilling a special well at their homestead. The well has its own water tank, which needs to be raised from the ground on rocks. Wendy and Scrambler arrive for the first ever time in Sunflower Valley..

programme.title: Bob the Builder
programme.desc: The Mayor decides that the town needs a drive-in cinema and Bob is asked to build it out in the countryside. The first film to be shown will be “The Invasion of the Giant Bug.”

programme.title: Homes and Playgrounds
programme.desc: Join Bob and his “Can-Do” crew as they go back on site to show you the exciting real world of big machines! From bird’s nests to houses, we all need a home and in this brand new adventure Bob explains how they are built.

programme.title: Bob the Builder
programme.desc: Farmer Pickles asks Bob to build him a roadside stall where he can sell ‘Pick Your Own’ produce. He has even had a Giant Carrot sign made to go on top of the stall to advertise his wares.

programme.title: Bob the Builder
programme.desc: Bob’s just finished tidying his mobile home when Farmer Pickles comes and asks if he’ll help with his Sunflower Harvest. He needs a silo built in which to store the ripe sunflowers before they’re taken to the factory.

programme.title: Bob the Builder
programme.desc: Mr Sabatini has decided to move from Bobsville to set up the Sunflower Valley bakery. Bob and the team are going to build him a new house close to the river. Muck asks if Spud can tag along as he’s been telling him exciting stories.

programme.title: Bob the Builder
programme.desc: Mr Fothergill has a nasty case of dry rot, so Bob and crew will have to use some smelly chemicals to fix it. As these will affect Mr Fothergill’s allergies, he goes to his sister’s for the day. But she doesn’t get on with Hamish, his pet parrot.

programme.title: Bob the Builder
programme.desc: Today Bob and the team have a very exciting job to do. They are building a bridge over the river to where Mr Sabatini’s new bakery will be. Bob promises Scrambler that he can be the first machine across the bridge.

programme.title: Bob the Builder
programme.desc: The whole town is practicing for an egg and spoon race, which Mrs Percival has organised. Trix decides to enter, but unfortunately she practices with fresh eggs and in her usual haste she manages to make a great mess in JJ’s yard.

programme.title: Bob the Builder
programme.desc: Scoop and Dizzy have spent the night at Bob’s old yard in Bobsville to help Wendy with some digging and mixing. The machines all wish that Wendy could come and live out in Sunflower Valley.

programme.title: Bob the Builder
programme.desc: Mr Bentley is organising a winter fair in the town hall square and enlists Bob to help set up the stalls, lights and the centrepiece of the fair – a giant inflatable Santa Claus.

programme.title: Bob the Builder
programme.desc: Scoop is keen to enter the town’s snowman competition, but a burst pipe outside Mr Sabatini’s needs fixing. Scoop is anxious when he sees the snowman that Mr Sabatini has made for the competition.

programme.title: Mega Machines: The Movie
programme.desc: Scoop, Muck and Lofty can’t wait to help Bob with his biggest build ever – building a dam and transforming an old quarry into a reservoir to provide Spring City with fresh water.

programme.title: Knights of Can-A-Lot
programme.desc: Bob, Wendy and the team get a big surprise when Bob's father Robert comes to visit. When stern Ms Chalmers from the National Heritage asks Bob to renovate the old castle a case of mistaken identity leaves Bob’s father in charge!

programme.title: Bob the Builder
programme.desc: Roley is desperate to help with the apple harvest, but there’s not much a road roller can do to help. Farmer Pickles, meanwhile, asks Bob to build him a new press to make apple juice. Roley tries to help Spud collect a load of apples

programme.title: Bob the Builder
programme.desc: It’s a blistering hot day on Sunflower Valley. Bob and Wendy have got to finish building their workshop – making the workbenches and installing the power tools – it’s an inside job so the machines all get the day off.

programme.title: Scrambler to the Rescue
programme.desc: Its winter in Sunflower Valley and Bob and his team are building a concert hall in time for a special winter celebration. Scrambler is especially keen to help – but is frustrated that there’s nothing for an energetic quad-bike to do!

programme.title: Bob the Builder
programme.desc: Scrambler is playing with the Sunflower Valley children; Sunny, Saffron, Carlo and Cassia in the playground. It’s the last day before they have to go back to school in Bobsville and they are all sad to be leaving.

programme.title: Bob the Builder
programme.desc: Bob’s built a new waterwheel for the old watermill. He’s made it at the yard, but now needs to dismantle it to take it to the mill in sections. Bob needs Benny, Scoop, Lofty and Muck to carry the wheel sections through the marshland to the mill.

programme.title: Bob the Builder
programme.desc: It’s the team’s first full day in Sunflower Valley and the machines are all a bit nervous of the mammoth job ahead. All except for Muck who is determined to show that he’s not a bit worried. Travis arrives with Bob’s new mobile home.

programme.title: Bob the Builder
programme.desc: The team are taking a break from building so they can tidy-up all the left over materials from around the Valley. Farmer Pickles asks Bob if he’ll put together Scruffty’s new kennel. Bob’s got a busy morning but agrees to build the kennel later on.

programme.title: Bob the Builder
programme.desc: Bob is building a water feature in Mrs Potts’ garden, while Wendy and Scoop are demolishing a dry stone wall. Bob and the Machines are greatly amused by the statue Mrs Potts has chosen for her centrepiece.

programme.title: Bob the Builder
programme.desc: Pam Goody is running out of money to complete the Arts Centre renovations and Molly suggests holding a fashion show to raise funds. Pam thinks this a wonderful idea and asks Molly to organise it. Bob is, of course, just the man to build a catwalk.

programme.title: Bob the Builder
programme.desc: Farmer Pickles wants a fence built around his compost heap, as Scruffty keeps getting into it. Muck is excited at the prospect of such a mucky job. JJ sends Trix up to the farm with the materials.

programme.title: Bob the Builder
programme.desc: Farmer Pickles is about to declare his Sunflower Oil business open, but there’s just one more job that he needs Bob to do. He needs a depot to store all the bottles of oil.

programme.title: Bob the Builder
programme.desc: Bob is building a marshland walkway with his Dad’s help. Benny hears that the marsh is ‘like a jungle’ and becomes determined to finds some jungle animals like tigers. Every time he spots one, however, it turns out to be something very ordinary.

programme.title: Roads and Bridges
programme.desc: For the first time ever, Bob is going On Site! Join Bob and his Can-Do crew as they explore real life construction sites. See diggers, mixers and trucks work together to build roads and bridges.

programme.title: Bob the Builder
programme.desc: Bob is building an extraordinary new cave-house for Sandy Beach – a caveman expert who’s moving to the valley to study how cavemen used to live. The team are all head into the mountains.

programme.title: Bob the Builder
programme.desc: Farmer Pickles has a new family of Pigs being delivered to his farm and Bob must build a new home for them. In the meantime, Farmer Pickles places the pigs in an old Barn to keep them safe.

programme.title: Bob the Builder
programme.desc: Scoop and the machines are in Sunflower Valley setting up Wendy’s caravan. Scoop contacts Muck to find out where Bob has got to. Muck explains that they haven’t left Bobsville yet because they’ve lost Bob’s Dad!

programme.title: Bob the Builder
programme.desc: It’s Mr and Mrs Bentley’s twenty fifth wedding anniversary and to celebrate Mr Bentley asks Bob and the gang to build a special summer house. Mrs Bentley is out for the day and the summer house is to be a surprise.

programme.title: Bob the Builder
programme.desc: Scrambler is desperate to help Bob build a drying barn for Farmer Pickles sunflower farm. Scruffty is also excited about the building job and runs and jumps about getting in everyone’s way.

programme.title: The Big Dino Dig
programme.desc: In this new episodic special Bob and the team tackle a big new job – building a dinosaur-themed fun park. With the help of park manager Tony Toberomi and new dumper truck Rubble, the team constructs a roller coaster, slide, train ride and flying ride.

programme.title: Crimes That Shook Britain: The Dunblane Massacre
programme.desc: In an emotional tribute, witnesses and victims' relatives look back on the tragic murder of 16 children and a teacher at Dunblane Primary School in Scotland, 15 years ago.

programme.title: Crimes That Shook Britain: Steven Wright
programme.desc: A closer look at the crimes of Steve Wright, who was dubbed 'The Suffolk Strangler' after murdering five women who worked as prostitutes in Ipswich in 2006.

programme.title: Crimes That Shook Britain: Wrongly Released
programme.desc: This special episode shines a light on criminals who have been brought to justice for one crime only to be released to commit further terrible acts.

programme.title: Crimes That Shook Britain: Shipman
programme.desc: Doctor Harold Shipman was convicted of killing 15 patients in 2000, but he is thought to have killed up to 215. Discover how he was eventually caught by detectives.

programme.title: Crimes That Shook Britain: John Sweeney
programme.desc: John Sweeney brutally murdered two of his girlfriends, dismembered their bodies and dumped them in canals. In a macabre twist, he hid clues in his abstract paintings, and five other girlfriends of his are currently still missing.

programme.title: Crimes That Shook Britain: Joanne Dennehy
programme.desc: Dermot Murnaghan returns to uncover the truth behind the horrific crimes of Peterborough serial killer Joanna Dennehy.

programme.title: Crimes That Shook Britain: Stephen Port
programme.desc: A chilling look at the crimes of Stephen Port, the east London serial killer who poisoned four young men with lethal doses of a date rape drug.

programme.title: Crimes That Shook Britain: Christopher Foster
programme.desc: Failed businessman Christopher Foster killed his wife and daughter before burning their mansion in Shropshire to the ground in August 2008.

programme.title: Crimes That Shook Britain: Ann Maguire
programme.desc: The tragic story of Ann Maguire, who was stabbed to death by a teenage pupil while teaching a Spanish lesson at Corpus Christi Catholic College in Leeds.

programme.title: Crimes That Shook Britain: Raoul Moat
programme.desc: The hunt for Raoul Moat was one of the largest in British history, after he wounded his ex-girlfriend, killed her new partner and shot and blinded police constable David Rathband.

programme.title: Crimes That Shook Britain: Stephen Lawrence
programme.desc: On the 20th anniversary of his death, the family of Stephen Lawrence reflect on his tragic murder in 1993, as well as their lengthy fight for justice.

programme.title: Crimes That Shook Britain: Lee Rigby
programme.desc: In broad daylight on a busy Woolwich street on 22nd May 2013, serving soldier Fusilier Lee Rigby was attacked and murdered by Islamic extremists Michael Adebolajo and Michael Adebowale.

programme.title: Crimes That Shook Britain: The Philpotts
programme.desc: When six children died in a house fire in Allenton, Derby in May 2012 it shocked the nation. Parents Mick and Mairead Philpott were charged with the murders. We uncover the reasons behind the arson attack on their own home.

programme.title: Crimes That Shook Britain: Colin Ireland
programme.desc: In the summer of 1993, gay men in London were being terrorised by a brutal killer. Colin Ireland, a loner from Southend, had made a macabre resolution – to become a serial killer.

programme.title: Crimes That Shook Britain: Jimmy Savile
programme.desc: Jimmy Savile: From national treasure to one of the UK's most notorious sex offenders: this is the story of how the revelation of Jimmy Savile's crimes left the UK reeling.

programme.title: Crimes That Shook Britain: Sarah Payne
programme.desc: Eight-year-old Sarah Payne's disappearance and murder shocked the nation. Here, Sarah's mum reveals her tireless campaign to protect other children from paedophilia.

programme.title: Crimes That Shook Britain: Raoul Moat
programme.desc: The hunt for Raoul Moat was one of the largest in British history, after he wounded his ex-girlfriend, killed her new partner and shot and blinded police constable David Rathband.

programme.title: Crimes That Shook Britain: Stephen Lawrence
programme.desc: On the 20th anniversary of his death, the family of Stephen Lawrence reflect on his tragic murder in 1993, as well as their lengthy fight for justice.

programme.title: Crimes That Shook Britain: Rochdale Groomers
programme.desc: Rochdale Groomers: Documentary examining the sex abuse scandal which occurred in the North of England and appeared to go undetected for quite some time.

programme.title: Crimes That Shook Britain: Rose West
programme.desc: With her husband Fred, Rose West brutally murdered ten girls between 1973 and 1979, making her one of the most high profile serial killers in the UK.

programme.title: Girlfriends: Adapt to Adopt
programme.desc: Maya and Darnell romanticize the idea of adopting until they attend a neighborhood group for people who have adopted children and are offered a baby girl to adopt.

programme.title: Girlfriends: Stand and Deliver
programme.desc: Joan goes to the school where Aaron used to teach in order to read a letter he sent to his former students. However, when Joan arrives, the students give her a hard time so Maya decides to use her street smarts to help Joan get through to the students.

programme.title: Girlfriends: What's Black-A-Lackin'?
programme.desc: Lynn gets upset when she learns that her record label is pushing back the release of her album to promote another artist, Chrisette Michele.

programme.title: Girlfriends: Toe Sucking
programme.desc: Four girlfriends: picky lawyer Joan, gold-digging real estate agent Toni, professional student Lynn, and Joan's headstrong assistant Maya get involved with each other's loves and lives, particularly after Toni starts dating Joan's ex.

programme.title: Girlfriends: One Night Stand
programme.desc: Joan, who hasn't had sex in a year, decides to have a one-night stand with handsome restaurateur Davis.

programme.title: Girlfriends: Hip-Ocracy
programme.desc: Joan, Toni, and Lynn decide to find dates on the Internet. When Joan meets Marcus she thinks he is too good to be true until she finds out he wears a girdle. Toni breaks it off with her date simply because he's too black.

programme.title: Girlfriends: I Pity The Fool
programme.desc: Joan's new boyfriend Marcus has been moving a bit too fast and she realizes her heart was not in the relationship in the first place.

programme.title: Girlfriends: Girlfrenzy
programme.desc: With Maya and Joan becoming close friends, Toni becomes jealous and feels she's losing her best friend.

programme.title: Girlfriends: The Importance of Being Frank
programme.desc: Joan starts dating her client's very attractive accountant Keith, only to be stalked by his estranged wife.

programme.title: Girlfriends: Everything Fishy Ain’t Fish
programme.desc: Joan learns that Maya's husband may be cheating on her and must face whether or not it's a good idea to tell her friend. Toni dates a City Councilman who can help further her career.

programme.title: Girlfriends: Never A Bridesmaid
programme.desc: Maya and her partner Darnell decide they want to renew their vows and have a formal wedding ceremony. Joan offers to help with the event and to have it at her home.

programme.title: Girlfriends: The Remains of the Date
programme.desc: Joan is determined to play the ultimate Halloween prank on William and succeeds. Meanwhile, Toni is left to deal with her dates strange sexual fetish for stuffed animals.

programme.title: Girlfriends: Fried Turkey
programme.desc: On a holiday that emphasizes togetherness, Joan struggles with feelings of loneliness and separation on Thanksgiving.

programme.title: Girlfriends: Pregnant Pause
programme.desc: Baby has never been in Joan's vocabulary until she gets the scare of her life and a new perspective on her priorities. As a result, the girls come together to give their love and support, showing their true Girlfriend friendship.

programme.title: Girlfriends: The List
programme.desc: Dreading another dateless weekend, the girls and William try Maya's suggestion to make a list of what they want in a mate.

programme.title: Girlfriends: They’ve Gotta Have It
programme.desc: Joan's new boyfriend Sean has not made a move on her after three weeks of dating and she is beginning to wonder why.

programme.title: Girlfriends: Bad Timing
programme.desc: Joan begins to question her relationship with Sean when he gives her a power drill for Valentine's and doesn't hear from him for three days.

programme.title: Girlfriends: Old Dog
programme.desc: Joan decides it is time to give in on her three-month rule and she is finally going to have sex with her boyfriend Sean.

programme.title: Girlfriends: The Declaration of Lynndependence
programme.desc: Joan and Toni decide that Lynn needs to learn to be independent. They give her a week to find a job, then she is on her own.

programme.title: Girlfriends: Friends, Colleagues, Brothers
programme.desc: Joan discovers that being the only black woman at her firm has many disadvantages and she is constantly ignored and overlooked.

programme.title: Girlfriends: Diss-regard
programme.desc: Maya checks into the hospital for her fibroid surgery and Joan has trouble managing her time between her boyfriend and her friends.

programme.title: Girlfriends: The Burning Vagina Monologues
programme.desc: Joan is stressing because Sean hasn't told her that he loves her.

programme.title: Girlfriends: A Kiss Before Lying
programme.desc: Sean notices that Joan always flirts with Davis. He gets jealous and tells Joan that she can never see Davis again or go back to 847.

programme.title: Girlfriends: Loose Lips Sink Relationships
programme.desc: Joan opens her mouth and tells Greg about Toni and Clay.

programme.title: Girlfriends: Jamaic-Up?
programme.desc: Joan takes the girls to the Sinbad Music Festival to help ease Toni to get over her breakup with Greg.

programme.title: Girlfriends: The Fallout
programme.desc: The ladies return home from their very eventful trip to Jamaica where Joan unintentionally ruined Toni's relationship.

programme.title: Girlfriends: Just Say No
programme.desc: Joan continues to suffer in her troubled friendship with Toni and her new relationship with her therapist, Dr. Bales.

programme.title: Girlfriends: A Full Court Conspiracy
programme.desc: Torn between her two worlds, Joan struggles with her professional duty while at the same time dealing with the guilt that she is not being true to herself or her beliefs.

programme.title: Girlfriends: Un-Treatable
programme.desc: Maya buys a dress that is too expensive knowing it might cause problems with her husband, Darnell. Joan treats herself to a trip to New York to visit her boyfriend, realizing the distance between the two of them is a problem.

programme.title: Girlfriends: Buh-Bye
programme.desc: Joan ends the friendship between her and Toni after finding out that Toni has been discussing her personal life with co-workers behind her back. Meanwhile, Lynn is trying to cope with her new living situation with Vosco, and his lack of a job.

programme.title: Girlfriends: Willie or Won’t He
programme.desc: Yvonne is concerned that William is never going to marry her because they have not set a date for the wedding. When Yvonne asks Joan for advice, Joan tries her best to stay out of it but ends up telling Yvonne to move in with William first to try it out.

programme.title: Girlfriends: Joan’s Birthday Suit
programme.desc: For her thirtieth birthday, Joan finds that she is happy with who she is, yet finds that she’s not so happy that her father is a nudist.

programme.title: Girlfriends: Trick or Truth?
programme.desc: When Toni loses her friend Joan, her man, and her gold card, it’s up to Maya and Lynn to help her get her life back together.

programme.title: Girlfriends: Maya Takes a Stan
programme.desc: Due to marital tensions at home, an emotionally fragile Maya finds herself falling into a fantasy affair with a handsome and empathetic new friend, Stan.

programme.title: Girlfriends: Mom’s the Word
programme.desc: Toni meets a great man but is dumped after she’s a bad influence on his eight-year-old daughter. Joan dates a movie star but is dumped when he begins to think she’s stalking him.

programme.title: Girlfriends: You Better Watch Out
programme.desc: Maya refuses to end her relationship with Stan, despite her girlfriends' repeated warnings to do so.

programme.title: Girlfriends: I Have a Dream House
programme.desc: As a gesture of his love, William buys a beautiful house for his fiance, Yvonne.

programme.title: Girlfriends: Sister, Sistah
programme.desc: Lynn's Caucasian sister, Tanya, travels to LA to help Lynn celebrate her Gotcha Day: the day when Lynn's childhood adoption to her Caucasian family became legal.

programme.title: Girlfriends: Willie or Won’t He II: The Last Chapter
programme.desc: The week of William and Yvonne's wedding is finally here and all goes smoothly until Yvonne discovers William never told his mother about Yvonne...ever.
programme.sub-title: Willie or Won’t He II: The Last Chapter

programme.title: Girlfriends: Can’t Stan Ya
programme.desc: After a horrid kiss, Maya finally realizes she must end her relationship with Stan. Afterward, however, Stan deviously becomes good friends with Maya’s husband, just to make Maya squirm as he says he may or may not tell her husband about them.

programme.title: Trump Unauthorized
programme.desc: A look at the life of real estate king/media mogul/reality TV star, and US President, Donald Trump.

programme.title: Phoebe in Wonderland
programme.desc: This heartfelt, fantastical story of a troubled, imaginative girl devoted to Alice in Wonderland explores the agonies of growing up as an outsider and the complexities of parenting. Starring: Elle Fanning, Patricia Clarkson, Felicity Huffman, Bill Pullman

programme.title: A Kid Like Jake
programme.desc: Parents, Alex and Greg are finding the right primary school for their son, Jake, a precocious four-year-old who happens to prefer Disney princesses to G.I. Joe.

programme.title: Barely Lethal
programme.desc: While on a mission, Megan Walsh, an assassin, gets intrigued with the lives of the other teenagers who are her age. She decides to fake her own death and enroll herself in a school. Starring: Jessica Alba, Samuel L. Jackson, Sophie Turner, Jaime King

programme.title: The Simple Life of Noah Dearborn
programme.desc: Academy Award winners Sidney Poitier and Dianne Wiest star with Mary-Louise Parker in this enchanting tale about a quiet hero who becomes the talk of the town.

programme.title: 17 Miracles: 17 Miracles
programme.desc: The pioneer trek across the plains of the Mormon pioneers.

programme.title: That Evening Sun
programme.desc: Academy Award nominee Hal Holbrook ("Into the Wild") stars as an aging Tennessee farmer who returns to his family farm, where he must confront a betrayal and the reappearance of an old enemy.

programme.title: Sweet Talker
programme.desc: A sweet-talking con man (Bryan Brown) starts to turn his life around when he falls in love in this family-oriented comedy.

programme.title: Dan in Real Life
programme.desc: In this down-to-earth dramedy, a widowed advice columnist and single father (Steve Carell) falls in love with a French beauty (Juliette Binoche) only to discover she is dating his brother.

programme.title: Evil Knievel
programme.desc: Bobby Knievel fulfils his dream of owning several car shops. Taking more and more risks in his stunts his popularity grows and he becomes the nation`s hero – the 'King of the Daredevils“.

programme.title: Count Duckula: Duck and the Broccoli Stalk
programme.desc: Duckula, Igor and Nanny find themselves up a broccoli-stalk and into a giant's castle.

programme.title: Count Duckula: A Family Reunion
programme.desc: Duckula hosts the family reunion but he does not like it one bit as he knows that his relatives will kill him if they discover that he's not a real vampire.

programme.title: Count Duckula: Ghostly Gold
programme.desc: Duckula travels to the black mountains in the Yukon to find treasure and gold.

programme.title: Count Duckula: Ducknapped
programme.desc: A ransom note is sent to Castle Duckula saying that Count Duckula has been kidnapped,.

programme.title: Count Duckula: The Lost Valley
programme.desc: Duckula, Igor and Nanny go to watch a film called 'The Lost Valley'.

programme.title: Count Duckula: The Incredible Shrinking Duck
programme.desc: Von Goosewing tries shrinking the castle to put in a glass snowglobe model.

programme.title: Count Duckula: Hi-Duck
programme.desc: Duckula, Igor and Nanny take an airplane to Nice.

programme.title: Count Duckula: Prime-Time Duck
programme.desc: Transylvanian Television arrive at the castle to feature it in a television programme.

programme.title: Count Duckula: Bloodsucking Fruit Bats of the Lower Amazon
programme.desc: After Fang the Bat's training by Igor as a blood-sucking bat is ruined by a preferred taste for choccy-biccies.
programme.sub-title: Bloodsucking Fruit Bats of the Lower Amazon

programme.title: Count Duckula: The Count and the Pauper
programme.desc: Duckula is sick of life at the castle and plans to leave Beckindale.

programme.title: Count Duckula: Arctic Circles
programme.desc: Duckula travels to the North Pole.

programme.title: Count Duckula: Transylvanian Take-Away
programme.desc: Gaston and Pierre and Von Goosewing break into Castle Duckula to steal two vases.

programme.title: Count Duckula: Whodunnit?
programme.desc: A letter arrives at Castle Duckula sent by lawyers in London.

programme.title: Count Duckula: No Yaks Please, We're Tibetan
programme.desc: The trio travel to Tibet

programme.title: Sabrina The Animated Series: Shrink to Fit
programme.desc: Sabrina and Chloe try to compete with Gem by fitting into the latest fad jeans.

programme.title: Sabrina The Animated Series: Nothin' Says Lovin' Like Something From a Coven
programme.desc: After being dissed from Gem's Halloween party, Sabrina throws a Halloween party of her own.
programme.sub-title: Nothin' Says Lovin' Like Something From a Coven

programme.title: Sabrina The Animated Series: Wag the Witch
programme.desc: Sabrina competes with Gem for office of Class President. They play dirty until Gem accuses her of being weird. Sabrina goes to disastrous lengths to prove her wrong.

programme.title: Sabrina The Animated Series: Witchy Grrrls
programme.desc: Sabrina's band, the Flavor Babes, use a little magic to launch their careers. The much desired fame and riches end up tearing Sabrina, Chloe, Hilda and Zelda apart.

programme.title: Sabrina The Animated Series: Paranormal Pi
programme.desc: When Pi sees Sabrina's Uncle Zamboni flying through the air he is convinced there are paranormal rumblings in Greendale. He makes it his mission to prove it to the townspeople.

programme.title: Sabrina The Animated Series: Anywhere But Here
programme.desc: Sabrina is tired of the restrictions that come with being a kid. She casts a spell that catapults into adulthood.

programme.title: Sabrina The Animated Series: Documagicary
programme.desc: Sabrina ends up in a new docu-series called, That Witch which becomes an overnight sensation! Sabrina soon tires of the demanding schedule and lack of privacy.

programme.title: Sabrina The Animated Series: The Grandparent Trap
programme.desc: When Sabrina hears that her Grandparents are coming for visit she is excited. They have a important announcement to make.

programme.title: Sabrina The Animated Series: I Got Glue Babe
programme.desc: Everyone is fed up with Sabrina and Gem's constant rivalry. Sabrina casts a spell to make Gem and herself closer. But she uses too much potion and it causes them to stick together.

programme.title: Tales from the Cryptkeeper: Transylvania Express
programme.desc: When surfer dudes Ben and Mike mistakenly travel to Austria instead of Australia, they find themselves aboard the midnight run of the Translyvania Express railway.

programme.title: Tales from the Cryptkeeper: Cold Blood Warm Hearts / The Spider and the Files
programme.desc: Kristin drives out to a small fishing community to investigate a local sighting of a sea monster. / Sixteen year old Simon's fascination with spiders becomes a nightmare when his turn of the century town is infested with arachnids of every variety.
programme.sub-title: Cold Blood Warm Hearts / The Spider and the Files

programme.title: Tales from the Cryptkeeper: Sharon Sharalike
programme.desc: Selfish eight year old Shauna doesn't like to share, especially with her five year old sister Katie.

programme.title: Tales from the Cryptkeeper: Too Cool for School
programme.desc: Junior High School friends Stephanie and Jan hate rules.

programme.title: Tales from the Cryptkeeper: Waste Not, Haunt Not
programme.desc: Richard and his younger brother Stephen, are willing to try anything to design the winning Science Fair project.

programme.title: Tales from the Cryptkeeper: Trouble in Store
programme.desc: Best friends Derek and Rob love new things.

programme.title: Tales from the Cryptkeeper: All Booked Up
programme.desc: Thirteen year old Greg Davis hates books.

programme.title: Tales from the Cryptkeeper: So Very Attractive
programme.desc: Fourteen year old Julia is obsessed with being beautiful.

programme.title: Tales from the Cryptkeeper: Drawn and Quartered
programme.desc: Aspiring artist Ralph Philips is focused on perfecting his art.

programme.title: Tales from the Cryptkeeper: Imaginary Friend
programme.desc: When Eight-year-old Mary Anne moves to a new neighborhood, she meets two boys who don't know the meaning of kindness.

programme.title: Tales from the Cryptkeeper: Town Gathering
programme.desc: Fourteen year old Erin has little else to do in her small town than to make up stories.

programme.title: Tales from the Cryptkeeper: It's For You
programme.desc: Gary is like any other 14-year old boy yearning for some privacy.

programme.title: Tales from the Cryptkeeper: Unpopular Mechanics
programme.desc: Randall loves to invent things, even if it means destroying other people's belongings.

programme.title: Count Duckula: Transylvanian Take-Away
programme.desc: Gaston and Pierre and Von Goosewing break into Castle Duckula to steal two vases.

programme.title: Count Duckula: Whodunnit?
programme.desc: A letter arrives at Castle Duckula sent by lawyers in London.

programme.title: Bitten: Bitten
programme.desc: Elena’s origins as a werewolf and the history of her love affair with Clay are revealed as she struggles to defend Clay against suspicions of murder.

programme.title: Bitten: Committed
programme.desc: Back in Toronto, Elena’s commitment to Philip and her human life is interrupted when Santos crashes Philip’s sister’s wedding party, offering her a way out from the strife of the Pack.

programme.title: Bitten: Stalking
programme.desc: With Santos revealed as the leader of the Mutt uprising, the Pack accepts his offer to discuss a truce. But when the meeting proves to be a trap, the Pack suffers heavy losses.

programme.title: Bitten: Prisoner
programme.desc: While Nick takes on the gut-wrenching task of erasing Antonio’s existence in the human world, and Jeremy is sidelined by with a strange poison.

programme.title: Bitten: Vengeance
programme.desc: When Santos enlists the notorious killer Jimmy Koenig to wipe out the Pack, everyone must put personal issues aside in order to survive.

programme.title: Bitten: Descent
programme.desc: Elena’s two worlds collide when Elena returns to Toronto for her art show and Clay is ordered to go with her for protection and surf on current lover Philip’s couch.

programme.title: Bitten: Settling
programme.desc: As Elena attempts damage control with jilted lover Philip, who is now aware of her romantic history with Clay, the Mutts launch a coordinated attack in Toronto.

programme.title: Bitten: Caged
programme.desc: Unwilling to lose anymore loved ones, Elena disobeys her Alpha’s commands and finally embrace the killer within in order to free a captured Clay.

programme.title: Bitten: Ready
programme.desc: Having learned the truth about Clay’s role in her werewolf origins, Elena reunites with the Pack at Stonehaven. Together, they prepare for a final stand against a Mutt army.

programme.title: 100 Years of Horror
programme.desc: Frankenstein is probably the most famous example of a man-made monster, but a great many other creepy creations have been fashioned by men.

programme.title: Colonel March of Scotland Yard: The Strange Event at Roman Fall
programme.desc: A wife reports her husband, a reclusive mystery writer, has fallen off a cliff. Suspicion points to her as the killer and Colonel March investigates.

programme.title: Colonel March of Scotland Yard: The Headless Hat
programme.desc: While on holiday in France, there is a murder at the docks and Colonel March is asked by a friend of the French police to assist.

programme.title: Colonel March of Scotland Yard: The Second Mona Lisa
programme.desc: The Second Mona Lisa,' painted six months after the first, is targeted by rival collectors. Colonel March is determined to reveal the truth.

programme.title: Colonel March of Scotland Yard: Death In Inner Space
programme.desc: A scientist seeks a way to suspend bodily life functions during space travel, and his test subject dies from lack of oxygen. Accident or foul play?

programme.title: Colonel March of Scotland Yard: The Talking Head
programme.desc: A 12 year old boy insists his dead father told him to kill his mother's new fiancée. But did the father truly die in an accidental plane crash?

programme.title: Colonel March of Scotland Yard: The Devil Sells His Soul
programme.desc: Colonel March hypnotizes five people to solve the stabbing murder of Lord Telford, while he himself portrays the murder victim.

programme.title: Colonel March of Scotland Yard: Murder Is Permanent
programme.desc: Colonel March investigates a murder in a beauty salon; there are plenty of suspects and it's up to him to single out the murderer.

programme.title: Colonel March of Scotland Yard: The Silent Vow
programme.desc: A customer dies from poisoned wine dining at a café, and the waiter falls under suspicion because he was a former chemist.

programme.title: Colonel March of Scotland Yard: Death and the Other Monkey
programme.desc: A cancer research scientist believes his test monkey was not only stolen, but also replaced. He is later found dead from gas; suicide or not?

programme.title: Colonel March of Scotland Yard: The Stolen Crime
programme.desc: A man demands to be arrested for murdering his wealthy wife whose death is attributed to natural causes. A valuable coin collection holds the answers.

programme.title: I Bury The Living
programme.desc: Cemetery director Robert Kraft discovers that by arbitrarily changing the status of plots from empty to occupied on the planogram causes the death of the plots' owners.

programme.title: Cell
programme.desc: Chaos descends when a mysterious pulse turns cell phone users into vicious predators. Desperate to reunite with his family, Clay teams up with survivors to battle the hordes of relentless killers! Feat: Samuel L. Jackson & John Cusack.

programme.title: After Life
programme.desc: After a horrific car accident, Anna (Christina Ricci) wakes up to find the funeral director (Liam Neeson) preparing her body for her funeral.

programme.title: Triangle
programme.desc: A group of friends suffer a yachting accident and take refuge on a cruise drifting on the open sea, but quickly realize they were better off on the upturned yacht. Starring: Liam Hemsworth, Melissa George, Bryan Probets, Michael Dorman, Joshua McIvor.

programme.title: Piranha
programme.desc: Doctor Baines has been conducting genetic experiments on piranhas and has made them virtually unstoppable. Unfortunately, Maggie, a private investigator, and Paul, an environmentalist, while searching for 2 people who disappeared in the area.

programme.title: The Bat
programme.desc: A crazed killer known as "The Bat" is on the loose in a mansion full of people.

programme.desc: Michael is assigned to a State Department mission that involves flushing out a political assassin at a Third World peace conference.

programme.title: Knight Rider

programme.title: Knight Rider
programme.desc: Michael infiltrates a ring of illegal gun traffickers. To gain their trust Michael poses as a potential buyer.

programme.title: Knight Rider
programme.desc: While a saboteur threatens the lives of several drivers in an alternative fuel race, Michael's investigative work is aided by a spunky journalist.
programme.sub-title: Give Me Liberty...Or Give Me Death

programme.title: Knight Rider
programme.desc: A magazine publisher working on a top-secret expose is murdered and it's up to Michael and the dead man's daughter to solve his mysterious death.

programme.title: Knight Rider
programme.desc: Michael pursues an elusive counterfeiter in a supposedly crime-free community, but is thwarted in his efforts by local authorities.

programme.title: Knight Rider
programme.desc: Someone is killing the great minds of the Helios intellectual society and its leader is determined to get to the bottom of it.

programme.title: Knight Rider
programme.desc: Michael wrestles with emotions from the past when he comes to the aid of a young woman he once loved, before he assumed his new identity.

programme.title: Knight Rider
programme.desc: Devon poses as a businessman while Michael investigates recent hijacking attempts against an alliance of local truckers.

programme.title: Knight Rider
programme.desc: Michael joins forces with a private investigator when a man who designs video games is suspected of selling company secrets.

programme.title: Knight Rider

programme.title: 2024 Simmons Bank Open for the Snedeker Foundation
programme.desc: Paul Peterson won by a single stroke for his first Korn Ferry Tour victory.

programme.title: The Starter
programme.desc: Catch up on all the latest happenings on the PGA TOUR with feature content on the best players in the world.

programme.title: The CUT
programme.desc: Check out all the latest content from highlights to behind-the-scenes features from around the PGA TOUR.

programme.title: PGA Tour Originals
programme.desc: Mark Immelman examines the golf swings from members of the 2025 European Ryder Cup Team.
programme.sub-title: Swing Theory - European Ryder Cup Team Edition

programme.title: PGA TOUR Backspin
programme.desc: Watch the latest highlights from the best players in the world from courses around the PGA TOUR.

programme.title: PGA TOUR Studios: Korn Ferry Presents In-Bound
programme.desc: Professional golfers strive to maintain their PGA TOUR status on the Korn Ferry Tour. The series explores the highs and lows of players' experience on tour.

programme.title: PGA Tour Originals
programme.desc: Go behind the scenes with all the exclusive footage around the historic 2025 edition of THE PLAYERS Championship.

programme.title: PGA Tour Originals
programme.desc: Delve into the inspiring journey of Rory McIlroy, one of golf's biggest stars. Viewers get a closer look at his life on and off the course, exploring the challenges he has faced and the triumphs he has celebrated.

programme.title: Keegan
programme.desc: A story of loyalty, resilience, and the unbreakable bond. When Keegan Bradley was left off the 2023 Ryder Cup team, it was his close-knit circle of friends-who helped him stay grounded. Two years later, Keegan returns as Ryder Cup Captain.

programme.title: The CUT
programme.desc: Check out all the latest content from highlights to behind-the-scenes features from around the PGA TOUR.

programme.title: Korn Ferry Presents
programme.desc: Spotlights the journey back to the PGA TOUR via the Korn Ferry Tour. Whether regaining Tour status after losing membership, or maintaining it after a comeback, this special explores how players have adapted to help them to a career on the PGA TOUR.

programme.title: Stewart Cink Wins the 2020 Safeway Open
programme.desc: Stewart Cink earned his 7th career victory on the PGA TOUR at the 2020 Safeway Open.

programme.title: PGA TOUR - The Drop
programme.desc: The Drop blends storytelling, competition, and personality. Each week, the show dives into the intensity of tournament play, previewing what's ahead and recapping the biggest storylines, giving fans a unique look at the players on and off the course.

programme.title: The CUT
programme.desc: Check out all the latest content from highlights to behind-the-scenes features from around the PGA TOUR.

programme.title: PGA TOUR Rewind
programme.desc: Watch the latest PGA TOUR champion's incredible shotmaking and winning moments as he takes home the trophy.

programme.title: 2024 Procore Championship - Final Round
programme.desc: Patton Kizzire won by five strokes to grab his first PGA TOUR win in over six years.

programme.title: 2023 Fortinet Championship - Final Round
programme.desc: Watch the final round of the 2023 Fortinet Championship, where Sahith Theegala shot a 4-under 68 to finish at 21-under par and secure his first win on the PGA TOUR.

programme.title: 2022 Fortinet Championship - Final Round
programme.desc: In the 2022 Fortinet Championship, Max Homa successfully defended his title in stunning fashion.
